programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Hunting the Debt Predators
programme.desc: Dispatches: With the cost of living soaring, reporter Ellie Flynn meets the everyday people pushed into exploitative and often dangerous situations with ruthless illegal moneylenders.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Wharfe House in Wetherby, run by B&B newcomer Pauline Cox, who is joined by best friend Claire Smith.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk where husband and wife team Jenny and Clive Aylett strive to be perfect.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit this week is to Bath Lodge Castle in Somerset where hosts Karen and Mike Hepker run a luxury castle experience.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport, Hampshire, where publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne like to mix business with pleasure.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the last day of the competition and the owners of Wharfe House, Wortwell Hall Barn, Bath Castle Lodge and The Old Lodge Hotel meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Liam and Patrycja kick off this week's competition at their Harboro Hotel in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire. They treat their guests to an afternoon of pie making, and two B&Bers intrigue everyone when they reveal their hospitality inexperience.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Nest Hotel in Hampshire, father and daughter Shane and Emily hope to dazzle with the décor. An afternoon spent at an ancient farm descends into mudslinging, literally. And the hosts end up feeling that they've been done dirty at feedback time.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Visit three this week is to The Queen's Head Inn in Northamptonshire, where B&B novices Simon and Harriet think their old-school village pub will be a hit. But the bacon gets a bashing at breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Worlington Hall in Suffolk, married couple Louis and Michael lay on luxurious interiors. Breakfast is an opulent affair and the B&Bers are left hailing their hosts. But one pair are perturbed by Louis and Michael's lavish approach.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers meet one last time on payment day to clash over cleanliness and spar over sleep scores, before the payments are revealed and we find out who's won.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karen and Paul want an apartment with lots of outside space to enjoy family getaways on Spain's stunning Mijas Costa. Scarlette Douglas shows them five options for their £145,000 budget.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mark wants a character property in the foothills of the spectacular Sierra Nevada with enough space for his son Luke to visit. Danni Menzies shows him what his £150,000 budget can buy.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, Kirstie's with Rob and Maria, who want out of their noisy flat and into a harmonious house, while Phil helps James and Lesley find a property that suits their future-proofing requirements.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie revisits Kathleen and Tom, whose Newcastle home was a battleground. When Phil last visited, they'd jumped ship. Have they got the renovation bug again?

programme.title: Yorkshire By The Sea
programme.desc: The 100-mile stretch of Yorkshire coastline is one of Britain's most popular tourist destinations, but life here's not all about beaches, buckets and spades. Meet the locals striving and thriving to earn an honest crust on the county's coast.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King is in Spain's Mar Menor to help Eileen from Birmingham find the perfect holiday home. Eileen ideally wants a two-bedroom apartment near the beach, and with a maximum budget of £43,500, Leah lines up five great options.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A new series from Woodgreen Pets Charity and more heart-warming tales of lost dog meets new owner. Can glam Poodle-cross Lola live up to the expectations of five-year-old Violet?

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britain's best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: Where the Crawdads Sing
programme.desc: When a man is found dead, a woman who lives by herself in the dangerous marshlands of North Carolina becomes the main suspect. As the case unfolds, the verdict as to what happened becomes increasingly unclear, threatening to reveal many secrets.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Pep and Malene plan to build a modern Viking Hall in the Surrey Hills. Following Pep's cancer diagnosis, it's a race to create a house that cherishes their memories and life together.

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: Agent Diana is set to look at a Mayfair property that could sell for a staggering £100 million when it is renovated, and there is already a buyer.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nation's roads.

programme.title: North to Alaska
programme.desc: A comic tale of love set in the gold fields of turn-of-the-century Alaska, where two prospectors have made a fortune. One is about to marry and while he puts the finishing touches to the honeymoon cabin, the other travels to Seattle to collect the bride. When the latter discovers that the girl has married someone else, he decides to find his partner another bride.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

When the latter discovers that the girl has married someone else, he decides to find his partner another bride.

programme.title: El Dorado
programme.desc: Gunslinger Cole Thornton rides into a small town to work for a cattle baron, but quits after learning that his would-be employer wants to drive a family off their land and obtain their water supply. Thornton leaves the situation unresolved, but a chance encounter takes him back to El Dorado, where he joins the sheriff in a range war.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: High Plains Drifter
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger rides into a small frontier town in the American southwest, provoked into a gunfight in which he kills three men, and then begged by the townsfolk to become sheriff. The residents are terrified of the gunslinger but live in greater fear of a gang which intends to wipe out the whole town.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Clips reveal whether a go-kart can withstand the power of a jet engine, how a plane can land on a busy freeway, what happens when you install a 240cc dirt bike engine inside a plastic toy car, and how tough terrain on two wheels can mean ride or die.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A family vacation becomes a life-or-death encounter, two trekkers cheat death, and an Amazon adventurer tries to save a dog.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A fisherman survives an encounter with hungry sharks, a motoring mishap turns into an aquatic emergency, a kite surfer is dazed, drowning and dragged along, and sea lions are not quite as cute as they seem.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A snowboarder gets stuck in a very perilous position and a cliff jumper survives against the odds.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Clip series showing just how deadly life can be. In moments of techno terror, see the result when you cross a bike with a drone and a helicopter.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Snowplough drivers and patrols are prepared to work all day and all night to keep UK roadways clear from obstruction when winter sweeps the nation. Fearless in the face of a cold front, these men and women are there to assist when disaster strikes.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Following the work of the North Yorkshire Police on the frontline of Britain's roads, where the fight against crime is fast and furious.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Eight police cars and the eye in the sky hunt down a high powered Porsche Cayenne that is causing carnage. Undertaking at high speeds and goading the interceptors, the driver even manages to take out several police cars.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Pursued by a helicopter and a team of armed response vehicles, two men armed with machetes and a sawn-off shotgun make a dramatic escape on a motorbike. Dog handler Duncan joins the firearms squad to close the area and help hunt the villains.

programme.title: The Cimarron Kid
programme.desc: After being wrongly accused of a payroll heist, the Kid heads for the high country where he joins a gang of outlaws, who are slaughtered during a a daylight bank robbery. Hiding out in a local ranch, he falls in love with the rancher's daughter, who tries to rehabilitate the cowboy.

programme.title: Man Without a Star
programme.desc: A rugged cowboy and an eager young farmhand encounter difficulties at the hands of their tough female boss. The pair find their manipulative employer has no scruples concerning the freedoms of neighbouring ranchers, and the two men decide to stand by their principles and fight back.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Rory risks life and limb to recovered a skip lorry from the M1. Jim has a happy reunion with his own truck. Chris has to find a way of separating two HGVs that have somehow become wedged together.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Traffic cop Dave McAllister and a team of other officers are on the M1 following a stolen HGV. Near a motorway service station on the M1, traffic cop Adam Shipley is on alert when a car triggers a police camera for possible offences.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Suzie Ranyard catches a man who claims that he has been driving with a dodgy number plate for the past eighteen years, a rowdy reveller is apprehended on New Year's Eve in Lincoln city and Jon Moon deals with an eager shoplifter.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A father receives a reprimand for dangerous driving on the school run, a wanted man using a false identity is apprehended and during a drive to clamp down on rural crime the Interceptors pursue some stolen tractors.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PCs Jamie Leslie and Jan Simlesa are teamed up in North Tyneside when they spot a shifty driver in a car worse for wear. In Sunderland, PC Mary-Ann Hutchison's cop car appears to spook an Audi driver into a last-minute turn and a near miss.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Taking Down the Shoplifting Gangs
programme.desc: Police piece together incriminating CCTV clips to tackle suspects linked to a foreign organised crime group. In Burnley, cops go after prolific shoplifting suspects whose campaign of shoplifting has got out of control.

programme.title: Highway Thru Hell
programme.desc: A local competitor challenges Jamie's status; a trailer full of cat food bursts into flames; Bruce has a fight and lands in the hospital.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: The agents investigate deaths and disappearances near a lake where small-town residents revive stories about a legendary killer sea-serpent.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A bunch of explorers get more than they bargained for, and a silverback gorilla gets too close for comfort.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Following the men and women of the heavy recovery business. In Hertfordshire, rookie op Euan is tasked with rescuing a ten-tonne rigid - one wrong move could end his rescue mission.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A toppled tanker loaded with liquid chicken guts needs rescuing, a refrigerated truck stranded on a busy road is leaking water everywhere, and an artic has smashed into two lampposts.

programme.desc: Conor Woodman visits Barcelona, one of the most popular tourist destinations in the world and home to a skilled set of conmen and thieves.

programme.desc: The Interceptors are outnumbered and outmuscled when they stop a coach load of cage fighters following an anonymous tip that there are drugs on board, and Kev and Andy pull over a Peugeot but the occupants are in no mood to hang around.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Hugs Rachel
programme.desc: Joey is upset to learn that Chandler plans to move out to live with Monica, but Rachel seems strangely unaffected by the news. Meanwhile, Phoebe pleads with Ross to either get an annulment or admit that he still loves Rachel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler disagree over how to redecorate Rachel's old room, and despite Phoebe's admonitions, Ross makes a drastic decision about Rachel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel forces Ross to terminate their marriage, Phoebe's first triplet babysitting experience proves stressful, and Joey pretends to own a German sports car.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One on the Last Night
programme.desc: It's the last night before Chandler moves into Monica's apartment, leaving Joey alone and prompting Rachel to make plans to move in with Phoebe.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, more than 1,000 drink drivers are arrested every year. On one busy road, officers search for a car reportedly swerving on a dual carriageway, and set a trap.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents a new run of the popular show. This time, ten police units from two forces pursuing a gang of car thieves, plus a helicopter joins another suspect hunt.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Police endeavours to tackle drunk or drug drivers. Police control receives reports of suspected drug driver. After the driver fails to stop, cops are forced into a high-speed pursuit.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers respond to a call reporting a house burglary and their stolen car. Minutes later, the traffic cops spot another stolen car using fake registration plates which they believe has been used by the thieves as a getaway car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors stop an uninsured driver with a violent past, Jon Peacock and police dog Brad hunt for suspected burglars, Sarah Capes gets a whiff of something illegal, PC Templeman pulls over a faulty white van and Dan Brigginshaw uncovers a driver's stash.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The hunt for a suspected burglar takes an unexpected turn, a late-night stop reveals a bag of drugs and Jon has a tussle with a fallen tree. Barry deals with a case of some very hot dogs locked inside a car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan is in hot pursuit of a two-wheeled tearaway, a suspected drunk driver nearly causes a calamity and Mark has to turn Colombo at a three-car collision. Meanwhile, Sarah has a communication meltdown with a Mercedes driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: In this episode, dog handler Martyn finds himself in the firing line when he cops a tasty right hook, Dan is given the runaround by an uninsured driver and Jon deals with a shoplifter who has been caught by members of the public.

programme.title: The Surgeon
programme.desc: We follow consultant brain surgeon Omar Pathmanaban at Salford Royal Hospital as he tackles a rare tumour that could be fatal, and also a complex and demanding procedure requiring surgery via the nose.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: 85-year-old John arrives with a head injury after falling from a ladder, 3-year old Leo has a burnt hand, and 65-year-old Ken is rushed in by ambulance after a serious car crash.

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A woman is rushed in after her motorbike collided with a deer. Strongman Adam is devastated when an accident in the gym means he may not be able to compete in Hull's strongest man.

programme.title: #TextMeWhenYouGetHome
programme.desc: The family of Kenia Monge share the agonizing details of what happened around the beautiful, 19-year-old's disappearance; her father, sister and authorities explain how the texts in Kenia's cell phone were the impetus to finding her abductor.

programme.title: My Lover My Killer
programme.desc: Katie Wilding's hopes for romance are shattered when Mitchell Richardson's dark and dangerous lifestyle consumes her.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Four top dermatologists treat patients for skin conditions such as cysts, lipomas and skin tags. Whilst the NHS views these surgeries as cosmetic these conditions can hugely affect the sufferers.

programme.title: Babies 24/7: The Maternity Ward
programme.desc: The reality of childbirth is laid bare when following expectant parents hoping for the perfect birth at The Royal Preston Hospital's award-winning Maternity Unit, where nearly 4500 babies are born every year.

programme.title: Babies 24/7: The Maternity Ward

programme.title: Walter & Dude

programme.title: Olly
programme.desc: Olly, a spirited three-year-old monkey and his toy friends, Kitty, Dolly, and Snail embark on various fun adventures together.

programme.title: Happy Birthday to You
programme.desc: Make your child a star on BabyTV by creating their very own personalized birthday greeting. Using a digital photo, parents can create a clip for their child which can be viewed online or submitted to BabyTV for the chance of being featured on-air.

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes
programme.desc: Come join the fun as Bambam mischievously tries to frighten Billy as she peacefully eats her curds and whey.

programme.title: Bath Tubbies
programme.desc: Ellie, Frogy and Ducky splash in the water and have adventures while playing in the bath. They float around, play with funny bubbles and invent new games with soap and shampoo bottles.

programme.title: Songs & Rhymes
programme.desc: When we meet somebody everywhere we go, we say hello. Hallo, namaste, annyeonghaseyo - Hello has different names - in German, Hindi or Korean. Let's greet our friends from all around the world with a nice hello in their language.

programme.title: Draco
programme.desc: Join Draco as he learns the difference between opposites - one and many. As he tries to figure out with how many balls he prefers to play, he has lots of fun learning the concept of one versus many.

programme.title: The World Is A Wonder
programme.desc: It's summer and a large bird with a long neck sings and hops on a tree! Autumn comes and the days get colder and so does the bird! Join the bird and his flock as they migrate to a warmer country! Come along and fly over land, lakes and deserts and build a new home on a nice tree!

programme.title: Zeina's Detective Agency
programme.desc: A highly familiar day-to-day object arrives at Zeina's doorstep forgetting what it is used for. Searching for the answer, Zeina and her best friend Roy come up with a new and hilarious use for the surprised object.

programme.title: Welcome to the Island
programme.desc: It's an exciting day on the island when a turtle and her numerous kids arrive on the island. In all the commotion, mama turtle finds herself flat on her back! Come see how she gets herself out of this predicament.

programme.title: Teeny and Tiny's Classroom
programme.desc: Teeny and Tiny are two happy and mischievous friends that share with us their riddles and games. As we follow the antics of the tiny friends, we discover shapes, colours and quantities.

programme.title: Mike's Bike
programme.desc: Mikey is the owner of a local bike store where he repairs his friends' bikes. Mikey always knows what to do, even if it takes him a few tries to get it right. In the process, he comes up with unique and funny ideas.

programme.title: Stick With Mick
programme.desc: Mick gets ready to go hiking. He grabs a pair of hiking boots and a water bottle.

programme.title: Crafty Rafty
programme.desc: Crafty Rafty creates an amazing choo choo train using a bucket, plates and a water bottle.

programme.title: Billy Bam Bam
programme.desc: Billy and Bambam learn how to mix colours in order to make new ones. When they finish painting, they use the colourful bottles as bowling pins.

programme.title: The Lollipops Song Series
programme.desc: Three toddler friends who form a girl band and turn everyday ups and downs into fun.

programme.title: Mice Builders

programme.title: Pix & Leo
programme.desc: Leo is all about getting clean but Pix is having lots of fun getting dirty. Leo is set on getting Pix cleaned up. Will he succeed?

programme.desc: Kim is a girl, Kit is her robot and together they have fun finding out about the world.

programme.title: Walter & Dude
programme.desc: Walter and Dude look for an item that is long and goes toot toot. In their interesting ways, the two curious friends discover if it is a trumpet, a balloon with a string, or a train.

programme.title: Mona & Sketch
programme.desc: Mona and Sketch set out on an adventure to become builders. Sketch scribbles a helmet, a cement mixer and a crane but they are missing a builder's suit.

programme.title: Hungry Henry
programme.desc: Henry wants to eat tasty strawberry pie but Chef George is out of strawberries. Join Hungry Henry on his journey to find strawberries. programme.desc: Billy and Bam Bam are playing the Tasting Game! All you need in order to play is a blindfold, boxes and some food! Come watch as Bam Bam tries to guess which foods Billy has in store for him.

programme.title: Dino & the Egg Hunt
programme.desc: Dino and his friend go in search of the two missing eggs. On their quest they climb a giant flower and search an ice cave.

programme.title: The Lollipops Song Series
programme.desc: Three toddler friends who form a girl band and turn everyday ups and downs into fun.

programme.title: Hungry Henry
programme.desc: Henry searches for his favourite dish and reveals how different foods are prepared.

programme.title: Mice Builders
programme.desc: Mice Builders NEW.

programme.title: Pix & Leo
programme.desc: Pix and Leo play hide and seek with their friends. Leo does a great job of finding everyone. But where is Pix? Will Leo be able to find her?

programme.title: My Robot & I
programme.desc: Kim, a curious girl, introduces her robot companion, Kit, to simple and fun activities. While she initially believes she is teaching him, she soon realises that Kit has lessons of his own, leading to a unique friendship.

programme.title: Walter & Dude
programme.desc: Walter and Dude are looking for an item that is hairy, has a stick and is used to clean and sweep the house. Is it a doll, soap or a flag? Join them as they discover it's a broom!

programme.title: Songs & Rhymes
programme.sub-title: Have You Seen the Baker Man?
programme.desc: What's more fun than eating cakes and breads? Eating them with your favorite BabyTV characters - Hungry Henry, Billy and Bambam, Draco and Oliver!

programme.title: Welcome to the Island
programme.desc: It's Coco's birthday and the gang have planned a surprise party for him! They even invited the Coral Reef singers! Now that's a real treat!

programme.title: Hungry Henry
programme.desc: Henry wants to eat guacamole but Chef George is out of avocados. Join Hungry Henry on his journey to find avocados. There aren't any at the grocery store or at the market, but there are avocados in the field, where they grow! Come along and enjoy good guacamole with Henry!

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes

programme.desc: Mr Snail learns a lot when he goes on his amazing adventures with his friends and neighbours.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: After rekindling her relationship with a former love (John Allen Nelson), C.J. (Pamela Denise Anderson) discovers that he has a serious disease.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: C.J. is pursued by a persistent executive who tries to impress her with his hot-air ballooning skills; Slade returns unexpectedly.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch is kidnapped by a woman who takes him to a fishing boat to treat her wounded husband, an escaped convict.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Hostage Mitch performs surgery on an ex-con; an environmental investigator has a near-fatal accident.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch agonizes over the near-drowning of a boy who lies in a coma.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch's upcoming birthday motivates him to enter an iron-man competition; Matt's effectiveness as a lifeguard suffers when he takes a night job.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: A murderer threatening to bring harm to some of the lifeguards; Mitch resorts to extraordinary means to bring an end to the situation peacefully.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch and the Baywatch lifeguards are asked by gymnast Mary Lou Retton to help organize an athletic competition for developmentally challenged youths.

programme.desc: The reunion of an elite group of lifeguards from the 1950s prompts Ben to make a decision about his future at Baywatch.

programme.desc: Stephanie and C.J. search for Mitch and his brother, who are lost at sea; Summer does some research on the ghost that has been appearing to her.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: An attractive woman and her plain sister vie for Mitch's affections; Matt confronts his fear of sharks.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch dates an investigative reporter whose story about an international government conspiracy has put her life in danger.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: The lifeguards help country music star Jessie Lee Harris (guest star Ricky Van Shelton) locate his estranged wife and son (guest stars Kassie Wesley, Brady Bluhm). Martina McBride also guest stars.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: As Mitch deals with budget cuts that could take lifeguards off the beach, Matt and Summer save a young couple from drowning.

programme.desc: Matt (David Charvet) becomes involved in a youthful con artist's scam; Stephanie's (Alexandra Paul) feelings for Scott (Phillip Troy) deepen.

programme.desc: Matt befriends a young boy who is ashamed of his father's dwarfism; Caroline's infatuation with Logan causes her to miss a rescue.

programme.desc: Mitch is annoyed by an arrogant FBI agent who is working undercover as a lifeguard in order to monitor a crime boss's girlfriend.

programme.desc: Nuair tha Bèibidh Dràgon Brot' a' cluich geama le Beag is Meanbh, tha e 'g ionnsachadh gu bheil co-roinneadh spòrsail. When Baby Soup Dragon plays with Small and Tiny, he learns that sharing is a lot of fun.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha Beag a' faighinn a-mach tòrr rudan mu Ghranaidh. Small finds out a lot of things about Granny.

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean
programme.desc: Oona wants to be a grown-up puffin and tries to teach Baba how to be an adult. However, she forgets the most important thing - young puffins need to have fun.

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean
programme.desc: When Oona, Mossy and Baba find a mysterious egg, they decide to try to find its parents. However, they have to work fast as the egg is close to hatching.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Tha Sula ri draoidheachd lainnreach ach tha Bing e fhèin son cothrom fhaighinn feuchainn. Sula is busy with sparkle magic and Bing, who doesn't want to miss out, tries to join in.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Tha Bing 's Pando a' farpais ach cò a chladhaicheas an toll as motha san t-sloc-gainmhich. Bing and Pando get a bit too competitive over who can dig the biggest hole in the sandpit.

programme.title: Peicein
programme.sub-title: Chan eil mi airson is gum bìd biastag eile mi!
programme.desc: Petit gets bitten by one of Tadeo's fleas. In response, he and his friends decide to create a food chain, from ants to mosquitoes to tarantulas, and get rid of the fleas once and for all.

programme.desc: Dave and his friends are enlisted as cheerleaders for their school sports team.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc

programme.title: Remembers.
programme.sub-title: Michael Palin Remembers... Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Few people have followed in Ernest Hemingway's footsteps quite like Michael Palin. In this fascinating interview, the much-loved broadcaster, writer and traveller reflects on his journey through the landscapes, cultures and stories that shaped one of the 20th century's most celebrated authors. From Hemingway's homes and favourite haunts to the adventures that inspired his greatest works, Michael shares the highlights of his travels, revealing what he learned about the man behind the legend.

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Exploring Ernest Hemingway's love of exotic places, including Spain, Kenya, the Green Hills of Africa and the snows of Kilimanjaro.

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: The places of Ernest Hemingway's youth, from his boyhood home in Chicago to the battlefields of Europe and the Parisian nightspots he frequented.

programme.title: Windrush: Portraits of a Generation
programme.desc: Marking the 75th anniversary of the arrival of HMT Empire Windrush to British shores in 1948, this documentary follows a remarkable project spearheaded by the King.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright hears from Harley, whose camera footage of his caravan being broken into was pivotal for the cops jailing a thief. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan takes a camper van for a spin armed with top tips to keep them safe. And we hear from a group of Doorbell Detectives who were the victims of a prolific porch-pincher on the rampage in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Owain almost gets lost trying to locate a flat in London. Martel's not a fan of a quirky design feature in York, and Jacqui is equally unimpressed by a makeshift construction outside a house in Wolverhampton.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Enid Bakewell MBE recalls overcoming prejudice and scoring a century in the first Women's Cricket World Cup in 1973 as tennis stars face cricket legends in a special episode.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: Detectives Damon Hollis and O'Neil Swaby are on high alert when a call comes in about a courier box from Canada, labelled as beauty supplies, that's been intercepted by customs. Inside, it's suspected to contain hidden drugs with serious resale value on the island. Crime scene investigator Tianna Mullan quickly begins photographing, fingerprinting, and collecting DNA, while the detectives gather evidence that could be crucial in court.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Scammers try to transfer a man's money directly to a Thai bank account. In desperation, the team dispatch the ground interceptors to the man's home address.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown heads to Suffolk with a couple from an East Sussex town who are utterly devoted to their three whippets and are desperate to leave their 1960s bungalow. They dream of running a small glamping escape, complete with a shepherd's hut for guests and a garden for their speedy, four-legged companions.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock heads back to Devizes to revisit one of the gardens she created with Chris Hull. John and Vanessa had wanted an Oman-inspired outdoor space perfect for entertaining, and four years on, Charlie is delighted to find a garden bursting with life and vibrant colour, with the fire pit and social area still at the heart of it all.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Bee and JJ go rummaging in Woking, helping a grieving widow sort through years of zoo memorabilia. Can gold sovereigns and a stone eagle fund a bedroom for Jan's great-niece?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: OnlyFans: Inside the Machine
programme.desc: Amber Haque reveals how OnlyFans models suffer widespread exploitation and coercion at the hands of their managers.

programme.title: A Simple Favour
programme.desc: Stephanie is a widowed, single mother who works as a vlogger in Connecticut. Her best friend, Emily, seems to have it all: a successful career, a loving family and a glamorous lifestyle. When Emily mysteriously disappears one day, Stephanie launches her own investigation by digging into her friend's past, finding a few surprises along the way.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end terrace in Manchester, a flat in Liverpool and a semi in Stoke-on-Trent are sold at auction and snapped up by developers hoping to make a profit on their investments. Martin, Dion and Jacqui follow their progress, which varies from a swift 20 days to more than four years.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The British Motor Museum plays host to the bargain hunters. Presenter Anita Manning leads proceedings, while experts Philip Serrel and Richard Madley help the teams find the bargains that will hopefully make them a profit at auction.

programme.desc: The Caribbean, where sailfish hunt with speed, sperm whales dive for squid and millions of red crabs migrate to the sea; hummingbirds defend territories post-hurricane, and on nearby islands, capuchin monkeys use tools to survive.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: The gang belatedly get involved in the Great Famine, the one thing they didn't need to get involved in. Callum's new job at the Ballymac Folk Museum sends him down a spiral of method acting as a starving peasant. And he's helped along in this grovelling subservience by his new boss, tyrannical land steward Phillip. The sudden news, to Jordan, that a million people died during the potato famine causes him to finally develop empathy, and it turns out to be a real pain.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Strange things are afoot in Ballymacnoose: animals in small clothing, paranormal sightings of leprechauns, and most bizarrely of all, Callum gets his first ever match on a dating app. The one prickle on the pickle is that Gwen, Callum's dream girl, is also in an open relationship with the 50-year-old Grainne, a woman who disembowels chickens for a living.

programme.title: A Simple Favour
programme.desc: Stephanie is a widowed, single mother who works as a vlogger in Connecticut. Her best friend, Emily, seems to have it all: a successful career, a loving family and a glamorous lifestyle. When Emily mysteriously disappears one day, Stephanie launches her own investigation by digging into her friend's past, finding a few surprises along the way.

programme.title: Chronicles of the Sperrins
programme.desc: Narrated by Colin Morgan, Chronicles of the Sperrins follows a year in the mountains and surrounding villages. This final episode was filmed throughout the Winter season As the year ends, the people of the Sperrins stay close to home and reflect, while in nature the cycle of the seasons begins again.

programme.desc: Chef Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond look at locally grown vegetables, cooking a spiced roast root vegetable curry with handmade chapatis. Joining them in the kitchen are cookery teacher Sumayya Usmani, who specialises in vegetarian dishes, and food and drink journalist Rosalind Erskine, who has brought along a drink made from Scottish potatoes.

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: The Bibby Sapphire's cook lifts the lid on the ship's galley and assistant life support technician Nancy Orton just how dangerous it can be taking food to the divers in their sealed chambers. A red carpet makes life difficult for events organiser Peter Ferguson for his arrangements in a boat naming ceremony. Pakistani-born Ali Akbar learns the ropes as a pilot in Aberdeen and barmaid Val Morrison joins the Celebrate Aberdeen Parade with her favourite charity.

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: Princess Anne makes the port chaplain's day while barmaid Val Morrison opens a local sandwich shop and later gets emotional when her favourite charity receives a windfall.

programme.title: The Agency: Unfiltered
programme.desc: Amy and Kirsten are on the hunt for a content creator with a huge following and a personality to match! This year, they also want to push themselves out of their comfort zone and find an influencer who has a niche that they have never previously represented. They have searched far and wide for the contestants to find academy candidates come from across the UK and Ireland.

programme.title: The Agency: Unfiltered
programme.desc: It's the second challenge at the academy, and at the start of the day, the competitors get feedback from Kirsten and Amy on how they got on in the first challenge. However, Cherry has halted proceedings by announcing that someone in the room has fake followers.

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: Michelle and Alan are making the last minute tidy-ups before the neighbours arrive for the housewarming of their freshly decorated home. Beth and Eric are first in the door, followed by Christine wearing an eye patch (from a rogue newspaper eye-scratching incident), with Colin and Cathy not far behind. Cathy is of course not backward in coming forward with less than complimentary comments about the couple's new look home.

programme.desc: A daring night mission causes group tension and isolates Pip. Jamie's case stalls until Pip decodes a clue that cracks it wide open. In court, the verdict is given.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: As they chase the truth, Pip and her friends face new risks and shocking discoveries. Will they find Jamie and make it through unharmed?

programme.title: Wedding Crashers
programme.desc: John Beckwith and Jeremy Grey are a pair of committed philanderers who sneak into weddings in a bid to pick up women.

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Kirsty Craib believes she has ADHD and has spent the last eight years trying to get assessed, without success. Many young women report experiencing the same struggles getting support for neurodiverse conditions. On a journey towards trying to have her condition recognised, Kirsty meets others whose delayed diagnosis has impacted their lives, and asks whether social media, while contributing to more awareness, is also making it more difficult to get help.

programme.title: Gaia: A Death on Dancing Ledge
programme.desc: This episode tells the story of Gaia going missing from her aunt's house just before sunset on a November evening in 2017. Just before she leaves, she makes a last-minute call to her identical twin sister, Maya, then she runs. She's alone without a coat, phone or medication for her epilepsy and in a state of high distress. After making several emergency calls to police, Gaia's family believe that a widespread search for her is underway.

programme.title: Gaia: A Death on Dancing Ledge
programme.desc: In episode two, Zara McDermott digs deeper into the world that Gaia unwittingly stepped into after she was approached online by an older man. Zara discovers how, in 2014, when Gaia was 16, she was approached by a man five years older than her online, who had a reputation in the local area. Despite multiple warnings from those closest to her, Gaia wanted to help him and they became close.

programme.title: Gaia: A Death on Dancing Ledge
programme.desc: Zara McDermott discovers that Gaia's alleged attacker contacted her ten months before she went missing. As her older sister Clara says, the texts were 'inherently threatening' and triggered a mental health crisis. As her attempts to get a restraining order were declined, Gaia spiralled. Within a month of receiving the texts, Gaia was in hospital, sectioned under the Mental Health Act for her own safety.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.sub-title: The Idaho Murders: Trial by TikTok
programme.desc: Zara McDermott heads to the USA and the small college town of Moscow, Idaho to find out about the tragic murder of four students and the global online manhunt that followed their deaths.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: Football's New Generation
programme.desc: The intimate story of England's newest recruits, charting their rise from grassroots heroes to Europe's biggest stage. Featuring Cole Palmer, Kobbie Mainoo, Ollie Watkins and Marc Guehi, the documentary reveals their personal stories for the first time, with contributions from their loved ones, family and friends, as well as the veterans who mentored them to the world stage.

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood takes an alphabetical journey through the world of gardening, getting inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular garden programmes. In this episode, she is exploring subjects that all begin with the letter G.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Beechgrove Garden is full of information for gardeners across the country who have to cope with the colder climates of the UK.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie is in East Sutherland to find out about the challenges of modern crofting, and Shahbaz visits the Bone Caves in Assynt to see how an increase in visitor numbers is damaging the site.

programme.title: MOTD FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Coverage of the Group C match between Haiti and Scotland from Boston Stadium. Scotland seek a winning start in their first World Cup for 28 years, while Haiti's previous appearance was in 1974.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt serves up a feast of food from Theo Randall, Ching-He Huang and Georgina Hayden. Meanwhile, Rick Stein and the Hairy Bikers head out of the studio.

programme.title: Strangers on a Train
programme.desc: Alfred Hitchcock's classic about two men who plot a perfect murder. When tennis pro Guy Haines meets smooth-talking Bruno Antony in the bar car of a train, he finds the stranger sympathetic with his desire to be rid of his clinging wife. The sinister Bruno then proposes that they swap murders: Bruno will murder Guy's wife, and Guy will do away with Bruno's father.

programme.title: Dial M for Murder
programme.desc: A playboy ex-tennis star arranges to have his wife killed, fearing that he is about to lose her - and her fortune - to another man. When the murder goes wrong, the ruthless husband is willing to do anything to save his own skin.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by iconic Hollywood director Alfred Hitchcock, reflecting on the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team travel up and down the country revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, as well as new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin visits the beautiful Cotswolds village of Broadway to uncover its artistic past.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Newtonmore, in the Cairngorms, Robert MacKenzie needs to gather in all his pregnant cows back to the croft for pregnancy testing. Costs of feed and fertiliser have shot up, so if not enough are in calf, it may be time to stop farming cattle.

programme.title: Expedition Killer Whale
programme.desc: A team of scientists and filmmakers heads to Antarctica to study the most sophisticated hunts in the animal kingdom - killer whales making waves to wash seals off floating ice.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: Every year millions of us flock to the homes and gardens of the National Trust. But out of sight of visitors lies a hidden world. Behind closed doors, dedicated teams are battling to keep priceless heritage alive.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve joins forces with shark scientist Mauricio Hoyos to discover where shark mothers give birth and help protect a new generation of these ocean giants in the Eastern Pacific.

programme.title: The Mother of All Cons
programme.desc: Meg's funeral is cancelled for a new autopsy, and the sleuths learn that there was no brain tumour. Meg's half-sister suspects their mother knows more and begins a search for answer.

programme.title: Later ... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: With Lola Young, Mike D 5D, Baby Rose, Wasia Project, Beverly Glenn-Copeland and Elizabeth Copeland, and Bonnie 'Prince' Billy.

programme.title: Children of Men
programme.desc: In England 2027, worldwide infertility has left the population on the brink of extinction and all hope of a cure is lost. However, underground activist Julian shows up with a miraculously pregnant girl.

programme.title: BlacKkKlansman
programme.desc: Ron Stallworth is the first African-American detective to serve in the Colorado Springs Police Department. Determined to make a name for himself, Stallworth bravely sets out on a dangerous mission: infiltrate and expose the Ku Klux Klan. The detective soon recruits a more seasoned colleague, Flip Zimmerman, into the undercover investigation of a lifetime. Together, they team up to take down the extremist hate group as the organization aims to sanitize its rhetoric to appeal to the mainstream.

programme.title: Southbank at 75: You Are Here
programme.desc: An epic dance spectacular to mark London's Southbank Centre's anniversary, forming an electrifying journey through 75 years of British youth culture.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve heads to the Moukalaba Doudou National Park in Gabon. Alongside primatologists and jungle experts, he wants to discover whether this unexplored rainforest could be home to an undiscovered population of chimpanzees.

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys question the value of a can of joke elephant manure and check out some signed presidential memoirs and a robot toy from the movie Forbidden Planet.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A banner from the WWII secret airborne division is brought into the shop, a customer brings in a 1980s Ooze-It toy, and Rick is muted by an unsigned illustration from the silent film era.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: When Chum gets his hands on a one-of-a-kind chair with a strange feature, will Rick turn a blind eye to his spending? Then, a helmet that once belonged to Evel Knievel lands in the shop.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A pair of vintage rock t-shirts; Gumby and Pokey toys; a poster by Israeli artist Gad Ullman; Corey and Chum look at unopened Atari games.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick tries to win one from the Gipper when a special pendant hits the counter. Can the patron saint of pawn shops help Rick land the deal? Next, a massive collection of vinyl LPs has Chum's head spinning.

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A collection of "Jurassic Park" toys comes into the shop that might not be as prized as Chumlee thinks.

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick and Corey discover a newfound love for an old Buick Roadmaster; Chum checks out a doll and volleyball signed by Tom Hanks; a physical bitcoin comes into the shop; a samurai sword lands on the counter.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick prepares for battle over a pair of vintage posters. Rick and Chum call on the power of Grayskull as they check out a large Battle Cat toy. Corey takes a look at an old fireman's megaphone. Chum heads over to a photo gallery.

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries

programme.title: Hazardous History With Henry Winkler

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.desc: A strange creature seen in a wooded area of rural Massachusetts by three different people who all give similar descriptions - coincidence, collusion or a genuine mystery?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: 500 years old, almost 300 pages long, full of handwritten notes and illustrations and so far no one can translate a word of it. What is the mysterious book and what secrets does it hold?

programme.title: Discovered By Disaster
programme.desc: A meteorite impact provides clues to a Biblical story. A sinkhole in Mexico reveals a tunnel full of treasures. And a medieval cave chapel becomes visible during a landslide in the UK.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. It's exciting at first, but when the wish ends, everything disappears. Rafi feels sad, until Doris shares a story about planting an apple tree when she was little. Rafi realises that nature likes to take its time, and that waiting can bring something special.

programme.sub-title: The One Where They're Up All Night
programme.desc: During their fist night together, Rachel and Tag realise they forgot to ship out an important package at the office.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's new job as a telemarketer gets off to a tricky start when the first person she calls, an office worker called Earl, tells her he feels unappreciated and wants to kill himself. Phoebe keeps him on the telephone, trying to persuade him otherwise and finally takes the desperate measure of going to his office to convince him to stay alive.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They All Turn Thirty
programme.desc: Celebrating Rachel's 30th birthday inspires the friends to reminisce about their own 30th birthdays. Joey got angry with God for breaking their deal, Ross bought himself a sports car in an attempt to recapture his youth, and Monica got blind drunk and embarrassed herself at her surprise party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Paul's the Man
programme.desc: Ross accidentally takes Elizabeth to the same cabin where Elizabeth's strict father takes Rachel for a romantic vacation, and Monica panics Chandler by planning their wedding.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Since she is the only one who knows that Chandler plans to ask Monica to marry him, Phoebe helps Chandler shop for a wedding ring, while Rachel's wish for Paul to reveal his emotions creates a new set of problems.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler's romantic marriage proposal is ruined when Monica's former boyfriend returns, Joey accidentally buys a boat, and a dormitory incident reminds Ross how young his girlfriend is.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is left reeling from Richard's declaration that he loves her and wants to marry her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler and Joey lose all their furniture, Monica finds that one date with her high-school heartthrob is more than enough, and Ross plays tricks on Rachel following their break-up. Elsewhere, Phoebe is temporarily convinced that a cat is the reincarnation of her dead adoptive mother Lily.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler has a brief fling with Rachel's boss Joanna which leaves him handcuffed without his trousers in her office. Meanwhile, Joey can only afford one book in an encyclopaedia set, and Monica caters a party for her mother.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Joey's New Girlfriend
programme.desc: Chandler falls for Joey's new date, Ross discovers that the woman that he thinks he's dating actually thinks he's the babysitter, Rachel's new college boyfriend steals, and Phoebe tries to re-catch a cold to improve her singing voice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Dirty Girl
programme.desc: Phoebe and Monica decide to go into business together after they cater a funeral where the widow tries not to pay, and Ross' date with the beautiful Cheryl goes awry when he kills a rat in her filthy apartment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Crosses the Line
programme.desc: Chandler finally confesses to his feelings for Kathy just after she splits up with Joey, Ross rediscovers his hopeless musical skills on the keyboard which Phoebe alone appreciates, and Chandler buys new furniture for the apartment since the robbery left them with just the canoe, chick and duck.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Chandler in a Box
programme.desc: The friends have a Thanksgiving meal together, Monica is reminded of Richard when she tries to kiss his son, and Joey frees Chandler from the box that he's being kept in to stop Kathy leaving him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's new job as a telemarketer gets off to a tricky start when the first person she calls, an office worker called Earl, tells her he feels unappreciated and wants to kill himself. Phoebe keeps him on the telephone, trying to persuade him otherwise and finally takes the desperate measure of going to his office to convince him to stay alive.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They All Turn Thirty
programme.desc: Celebrating Rachel's 30th birthday inspires the friends to reminisce about their own 30th birthdays. Joey got angry with God for breaking their deal, Ross bought himself a sports car in an attempt to recapture his youth, and Monica got blind drunk and embarrassed herself at her surprise party.

programme.title: The Daily Show
programme.desc: 'The Daily Show' is back with a new host but the same irreverent take on the fools, fibbers and various other f-words in today's headlines.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert is seeing a 22-year-old girl, and Debra and Marie think he's being foolish, but Ray and Frank aren't so sure. The question is, is she really 22?

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra's parents come round for Thanksgiving dinner. Debra's so pleased that they're not like Frank and Marie - or are they?

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie mollycoddles Ray when he thinks he's coming down with something, much to Debra's despair.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray buys Debra an elaborate Christmas present in the hope that she will consequently let him go on a golfing weekend, but he hasn't anticipated the present she's getting him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger, while Chandler can't remember which of Joey's many attractive sisters he kissed at a party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Ross Says Rachel
programme.desc: Ross has married Emily, but during the reception she punches him and flees. Meanwhile, new lovers Monica and Chandler are finding it hard to keep their agreement.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Joey's New Brain
programme.desc: Joey accidentally informs a cast member that she will be fired so that his character will get her character's brain, and Ross plans a surprise for Monica and Chandler's wedding - he wants to play the bagpipes.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Truth About London
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler fondly reminisce about London until a startling revelation leaves Chandler floored. Meanwhile, Rachel teaches Ben practical jokes to play on Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Cheap Wedding Dress
programme.desc: Monica regrets telling a fellow bride-to-be about a cheaper bridal shop, and later she has to fight the woman tooth and nail for a unique wedding dress before competing with her to hire Chandler's favourite band for the night. Meanwhile, Joey and Ross discover that they're dating the same girl.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is overjoyed to be nominated for a `Soapie', the third most prestigious award in daytime dramas. Meanwhile, engaged Monica frets when she realises she will never experience the thrill of a new relationship again, Ross spots Chandler sneaking out of a male strip club, and Phoebe meets a cute guy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross and Monica's Cousin
programme.desc: Monica is so perturbed by Chandler's instant attraction to her gorgeous visiting cousin that she persuades Ross to let her stay at his apartment. Despite being her blood relative, Ross cannot restrain himself from attempting to kiss her. She escapes to Phoebe's apartment, but her beauty flabbergasts even Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Big Kiss
programme.desc: Rachel runs into an old sorority sister with whom she once shared a passionate encounter.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: With only four weeks until their wedding, Monica and Chandler try to write their vows. Chandler enlists the boys' help, while Monica does the same with the girls.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica convinces a reluctant Chandler to travel to Las Vegas to invite his estranged cross-dressing father to their wedding. Meanwhile, Rachel is furious that she is the only friend who Monica has not allowed to drive the Porsche that her father gave her.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Presented in front of a live audience at the Theatre Royal on Drury Lane. McIntyre brings his own comedy to the stage, as well as introducing celebrity guests, musicians and other fellow comedians to perform alongside him.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Jess continues to have great sex with Sam, but does not date. She feels uncomfortable with a sex only relationship. Nick becomes her guy to date, but with no sex.

programme.title: Jack Dee: Live At The Apollo
programme.desc: He's glum. He wears nice suits. And he's back. Deadpan comic Jack Dee captured in live performance at London's Hammersmith Apollo.

programme.title: Fat Chat
programme.desc: Desiree Burch welcomes comedian Sofie Hagen into her dreamy diner. Together they lament the struggles of plane seatbelt extenders and discuss Sofie hooking up with someone her neighbour.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel must hire an assistant and agonises over whether to hire an efficient, experienced woman, or sexy young Tag Jones. Ignoring her friends' advice, she hires Tag and soon regrets it. To make matters worse, Tag is attracted to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Engagement Picture
programme.desc: While taking an engagement picture for himself and Monica, Chandler can't stop pulling a strange face, Rachel enlists Joey to take Tag out for a night on the town, and Phoebe and Ross date a couple in the middle of a divorce.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Nap Partners
programme.desc: Wedding fever continues when Monica can't decide whether to appoint Rachel or Phoebe as her maid of honour. To help Monica decide, Joey and Ross test them on how they would react in certain wedding situations.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross's Library Book
programme.desc: Ross is elated that the university library contains his doctoral dissertation, but he is shocked to discover that it is located in a remote corner frequently used by students having sex. Elsewhere, the tables are turned on Joey when Erin is the one to just want sex and not a relationship.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Doesn't Like Dogs
programme.desc: Joey may have ruined Rachel's chances with Tag when he opens his mouth during Thanksgiving dinner, and Phoebe reveals that she has been secretly keeping a cute dog in her room for a few days while its owner is away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Candy
programme.desc: Rachel is dating her assistant, Tag, but they must keep it a secret at work because of the company's anti-dating policy. Her friends warn her that the relationship will only cause her trouble, but just for fun, she becomes more brazen about it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger, while Chandler can't remember which of Joey's many attractive sisters he kissed at a party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Ross Says Rachel
programme.desc: Ross has married Emily, but during the reception she punches him and flees. Meanwhile, new lovers Monica and Chandler are finding it hard to keep their agreement.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel from learning his disturbing secret. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Joey spend a night eavesdropping.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel's attempt to explain how they wound up in bed together quickly descends into an argument over who seduced who, until Ross shocks the room by announcing that he has the whole encounter on video. Meanwhile, Monica and Chandler return from their honeymoon, excited at the prospect of socialising with another just-married couple they met on the flight home.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Princess Leia Fantasy
programme.desc: Monica is distraught after her break-up with Richard. Phoebe unsuccessfully tries to raise her spirits, but ultimately it's Mr Geller who comforts his daughter. Elsewhere, Chandler and Janice begin seeing each other again and Joey can't stand it. The two plan a day of fun to try to overcome Joey's aversions for Chandler's sake. Meanwhile, Rachel tells Phoebe and Monica about Ross's Princess Leia fantasy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Evil Orthodontist
programme.desc: Rachel gets back together with her ex-fiancé Barry, even though he's engaged to her best friend Mindy. Meanwhile, Chandler agonises over a wonderful woman who fails to call him after their first date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Holiday Armadillo
programme.desc: Ross unexpectedly finds himself looking after Ben and decides to do something special for Christmas. Since all the Santa costumes have been rented out, Ross dons the best outfit left and entertains Ben as the holiday armadillo.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Cheesecakes
programme.desc: Chandler and Rachel pig out on a neighbour's cheesecake that was mistakenly delivered to Chandler. They feel bad, but not quite guilty enough to refrain from eating a second one that has also been wrongly delivered.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They're Up All Night
programme.desc: During their fist night together, Rachel and Tag realise they forgot to ship out an important package at the office.

programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Big Kiss
programme.desc: Rachel runs into an old sorority sister with whom she once shared a passionate encounter.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes have reached the final stop on their Latin America road trip as they arrive in Panama - they meet some local indigenous tribes, but things gets competitive between father and son as they join in with some ancestral games. When the pair reach Panama's capital city, the skyscraper skyline is a big clue to what Barney has in store for his dad - and Brad isn't pleased when he finds out that they'll be cleaning the windows of one of those skyscrapers.

programme.title: Three Men and a Little Lady
programme.desc: The sequel to the popular `Three Men and a Baby' sees the three dads living in domestic harmony with the now five-year-old Mary and her mother Sylvia. When Sylvia announces she is moving to London to marry her English boyfriend, everything is thrown into turmoil and the surrogate fathers decide to cross the Atlantic - determined to stop the wedding.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Absurd comedy about a likeable buffoon who turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment. Mr Bean rushes to his exam only to find out he's studied the wrong subject. He then goes to the beach but struggles to change into his swimming trunks discretely. Later on, he has difficulty attending a church service without drawing attention to himself.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean wants to give a busker some money until he realises he has no change, while afterwards he goes shopping but gets carried away. Later, he treats himself to a birthday dinner but is disappointed with his dish. Finally, Mr Bean attends a royal film premiere in order to meet the Queen, with disastrous results.

programme.title: Comedy Central Presents
programme.desc: Comic Jimmy Carr discusses various topics including being British and performs his unique style of comedy.

programme.title: Comedy Central Presents
programme.desc: Comic Jimmy Carr performs using his sharp wit and dirty mind while slowly showing his mild-mannered demeanour.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: With Sarah Millican, Micky Flanagan, Georgie Thompson and Louie Spence.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: The first of a two-part compilation of the best bits from the current series. Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson play the topical comedy panel show that delves into the world of polls, surveys and statistics in its own unique way. Jimmy oversees the disputes and deliberations on the current events that set the nation's chins wagging with celebrity guests.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha, Wayne, Colin, and Ryan are joined by comedian Jeff Davis for a series of hilarious games, including Questions with Hats, Sound Effects, Scenes from a Hat, Living Scenery, and Hoedown. The Bella twins arrive for a round of Duet.

When Sylvia announces she is moving to London to marry her English boyfriend, everything is thrown into turmoil and the surrogate fathers decide to cross the Atlantic - determined to stop the wedding.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Absurd comedy about a likeable buffoon who turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment. Mr Bean rushes to his exam only to find out he's studied the wrong subject. He then goes to the beach but struggles to change into his swimming trunks discretely. Later on, he has difficulty attending a church service without drawing attention to himself.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean wants to give a busker some money until he realises he has no change, while afterwards he goes shopping but gets carried away. Later, he treats himself to a birthday dinner but is disappointed with his dish. Finally, Mr Bean attends a royal film premiere in order to meet the Queen, with disastrous results.

programme.title: Comedy Central Presents
programme.desc: Comic Jimmy Carr discusses various topics including being British and performs his unique style of comedy.

programme.title: Comedy Central Presents
programme.desc: Comic Jimmy Carr performs using his sharp wit and dirty mind while slowly showing his mild-mannered demeanour.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: With Sarah Millican, Micky Flanagan, Georgie Thompson and Louie Spence.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: The first of a two-part compilation of the best bits from the current series. Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson play the topical comedy panel show that delves into the world of polls, surveys and statistics in its own unique way. Jimmy oversees the disputes and deliberations on the current events that set the nation's chins wagging with celebrity guests.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha, Wayne, Colin, and Ryan are joined by comedian Jeff Davis for a series of hilarious games, including Questions with Hats, Sound Effects, Scenes from a Hat, Living Scenery, and Hoedown. The Bella twins arrive for a round of Duet.

programme.title: Lee Evans: The Ultimate Experience
programme.desc: All the best of Lee Evans Live, rolled into one delicious whole show.

programme.title: Comedy Central Live
programme.desc: Iain Stirling performs live from Camden in the first of nine stand-up specials. With brand new material, Iain talks about growing old and his previous work as a children's TV presenter.

programme.title: The Pep Talk
programme.desc: Nothing makes you better at succeeding than failing. KG Tha Comedian inspires you to become a better you, by embracing the moments you are a failing you. FAILURE = SUCCESS.

programme.title: Utopia
programme.desc: The Promise Land. The Government stalls the NBA moving to independent status and the office undergoes respect training, complicating a romance between Nat and a visiting consultant. (S5, E03)

programme.title: Teachers
programme.desc: Mrs Adler joins a children's music group and is too ashamed to tell the other teachers. Ms Watson and Ms Bennigan find their classes passing notes, which causes a rift in their friendship.

programme.title: Teachers
programme.desc: Mrs Adler joins a children's music group and is too ashamed to tell the other teachers. Ms Watson and Ms Bennigan find their classes passing notes, which causes a rift in their friendship.

programme.title: Stupid Central
programme.desc: Comedian Babatunde Aléshé delivers a fresh batch of the funniest clips. This episode's family-friendly fails including epic bad hair days, roller-skating gone wrong and what not to do on holiday!

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn
programme.desc: After a teenager mysteriously disappears, police must determine if a secret meeting with a young man led to her murder.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn
programme.desc: When a teenager is found savagely stabbed to death in her home, police hope a trail of blood found at the crime scene will lead them to her killer.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn
programme.desc: A young woman's disappearance marks the beginning of a heartbreaking murder mystery. Will a clue discovered by a child be the key to catching his mother's killer?

programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan explores British murder stories filled with detective intrigue and chilling storylines of homicide and hate. The five murder stories are told with archived footage and key interviews from those closest to the case.

programme.title: Cops Gone Bad With Will Mellor
programme.desc: In the middle of the night, police and paramedics are called to what they are told is a devastating car accident. A young woman is in the driver's seat with horrific head injuries, yet her fiancé, police officer Martin Alex Forshaw, is barely hurt.

programme.title: Cops Gone Bad With Will Mellor
programme.desc: A police car executes a stop and search on a drugs courier. The cop who stops the car finds a bag with hundreds of thousands of pounds inside and seizes it as the proceeds of crime. Instead of arresting the driver, the police officer lets him go.

programme.title: #Dead2Me
programme.desc: #Dead2Me tells the stories of the love between two people which turns to loathing - and ends in Murder. Ours is a narrative fuelled by a tapestry of emotions. The stories are told by those who knew a killer without realising it.

programme.title: #Dead2Me
programme.desc: Dawn Walker met Thomas Nutt online, and from the very beginning he tried to control every aspect of her life. He manipulated her into marriage, and their wedding day was the last time Dawn's family would see her alive.

programme.title: When Missing Turns to Murder
programme.desc: Chronicling the desperate search for Carole Packman, who disappeared in 1985 after leaving a note to say she was leaving the family home for good. With the help of the police, they discover her disappearance wasn't as honest as it was first believed.

programme.title: When Missing Turns to Murder
programme.desc: Danielle Jones, aged 15, was last seen on June 18, 2001, near her home in East Tilbury, Essex. Her uncle became a suspect, where a search of his house led police to a pair of white stockings stained with blood. Her mother speaks about her murder.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: An account of the car bomb carried out by the IRA which killed 29 people in Omagh on 15 August 1998.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: A look at the murder of 18-year-old Sally Anne Bowman, who was stabbed in Croydon in 2005. With interviews with Bowman's family and detectives who solved the case.

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: Detectives hope a woman can identify her attacker as she fights for her life after being shot in the face, and investigators race to get a confession after a man is gunned down in a motel car park.

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: A young woman vanishes after a night out at a local bar and investigators race against the clock to track down her final movements, eventually locating a potential suspect who is anything but honest.

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: After the unusual death of a local businessman, police become suspicious of his disgruntled wife, whose strange behaviour and statements make her the prime suspect in the shooting.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: Though wedded couples vow to stay together until death do they part, a look at what happens when a groom or bride causes their beloved's death.

programme.title: Cold Case Files: Dead West
programme.desc: When Pam Shelly, 31, is found in her Cuero, Texas bathroom with a fatal gunshot wound to the head, her death is initially ruled a suicide. Yet, a tenacious investigator's instincts and his suspicion of foul play eventually cracks the case wide open.

programme.title: The Death Investigator With Barbara Butcher
programme.desc: When a rubbish bag containing a young woman's body is dumped in a NYC tunnel, Barbara Butcher and a rookie homicide detective unravel how a seemingly straightforward crime conceals a chain of forensic contradictions - and a killer no one saw coming.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: 23-year-old teacher Irene Garza was a former beauty queen and devout Catholic who vanished the evening before Easter. Residents of her close-knit border town did their best to find her, but her body was found floating in a drainage canal.

programme.title: Crime in Progress
programme.desc: A routine mail route turns deadly when a beloved postal worker is murdered in broad daylight. With body-worn cameras rolling, police and federal agents race to uncover the truth - and what they find leads to shocking arrests.

programme.title: The Simpsons
