programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Lowell Hawthorne, President and CEO of Caribbean food producers Golden Krust, dons a disguise to go undercover among the workforce in his own company.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: A Los Angeles auction attracts more buyers than Dan and Laura anticipated; Dave Hester returns to action; Ivy attempts to keep the peace; Rene's father, Gunter, joins the festivities.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy and Dave fight for a pair of units in Moreno Valley, Calif. Mavrick seeks a quality locker at a fair price, and one lucky buyer scores a find that brings over $10,000 in profit.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In and around Nottingham, young lovebirds Gabz and Pete kick off the competition by cooking the food they fell in love eating together. They go up against Sushma and Gulab and country-dwelling partners John and Shaun.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, young lovebirds Andy and Pim are hoping to impress their guests with a Scottish and Thai-themed menu, with a bit of American thrown in for good measure.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride Ireland
programme.desc: Apart from being head over heels in love with his blushing bride Lisa Toner, groom Owen Walsh is a self-professed nerd. The big loves in his life are: Doctor Who, Star Wars and the fantasy TV series, Game of Thrones.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride Ireland
programme.desc: For bride Celina who doesn't do a bad hair day, will groom Ben tick all the right boxes with hair, make up and the perfect wedding dress for his bride with a growing bump?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Recording artist Darius Rucker travels to Austin, Texas, where he goes undercover to run an open mic night, work as a roadie and walk the city's famous Sixth Street in search of musical talent.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur Marcus Samuelsson goes undercover at a public cooking class, a culinary institute, a high-end food truck and a soup kitchen to find and mentor new culinary talent.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: In this episode, Gordon Ramsay travels to Wichita, Kansas in an attempt to save Fetch Bistro, the first dog-friendly restaurant in the state.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: At Hermosa Beach in California, Gordon attempts to save Patrick Molloy's, a family-owned Irish pub, by refurbishing the dining room and creating a simplified and delicious menu.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The buyers look for south-of-the-border treasure in San Diego, Rene and Casey defend their home turf against Darrell and Brandon, and Mary eyes a high-end household unit.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The buyers dig deep for bargains in the desert country of Mojave, California. Dave works hard despite his dislike of the territory, and Ivy hunts for fun off-road finds.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Durham, Steph and James plan a gastropub feast - if they can avoid falling out. Gary and Amanda's pig drops in on their night, and on night three the guests discover Andy's reward chart.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The first team task of the series brings the usual awkwardness, but also the joys of Stevie Martin inventing a new way for humans to move, and Fatiha El-Ghorri sweeping the lawn with a small broom.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with Sean Lock and David Mitchell going up against Jon Richardson and Katherine Ryan. Brett Domino Trio joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: The boys are threatened with no crumble if they don't help to assemble a wooden shelf, while Jim's dog Wilson keeps doing his 'dollops' in Mum and Dad's flowerbed.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Dad's bought a grotty caravan. Jim's got a new dog. And the boys have new `females', but they are unsure whether they should tell mum.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark makes a final play for Dobby, the object of his desires, while Jeremy meets Elena, a Russian émigré who loves music and poetry. Both men are determined to impress, but one of them is an expectant father. When the pregnant Sophie spills the beans, both Mark and Jeremy's worlds change forever; worse still, the new boiler is playing up.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Jeremy realises that he's in love with Elena and decides to be generally less selfish to win her affection. He also thinks it might be a good idea to tell her he loves her, although Mark isn't so sure. Meanwhile, Dobby encourages Mark to become a history walk tour guide.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers on Holiday
programme.desc: This week, the pop-up tattoo parlour in the sun gets a visit from Wez, who needs help hiding his hideous Kardashian catastrophe, while Alice gives Lana's silly emoji a facelift and Glen sorts out Joey's truly offensive nipple tattoo.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: It may be his birthday but poor Manny isn't in the mood to celebrate. Meanwhile, Phil and Claire engage in a car race, while Mitch and Cam get sidetracked.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell isn't sure how to tell Cam that his mother makes him feel uncomfortable, Haley finally breaks up with Dylan and Jay has a little stomach ache.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: While Jay continues to adjust to life with his young Latino bride and her sensitive son, Claire tries to keep a leash on husband Phil and their brood, and gay couple Mitch and Cam experience the marvels and mayhem of bringing up their adopted Vietnamese baby.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: What happens when men are left in charge to plan a surprise wedding for their partners?

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: At Sacramento in California, Gordon helps out BBQ and seafood restaurant Sandra Dee's. He and his team have just 24 hours to renovate the premises by updating the 20-year-old dining room's faded decor and overhauling the kitchen and menu.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon updates the interior of Shanty on 19th, an iconic restaurant in the West End theatre district of Allentown, Pennsylvania, as a bright and airy contemporary bistro.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa meets the son of the late musician Bleeding Gums Murphy -- and attempts to improve his life.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Dante's Peak

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Grampa finds himself in a pickle when his hidden hamburger past comes back to haunt him.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Venom

programme.desc: Lt Frank Hewitt deserts the Union army to warn of an impending Indian attack on the Texan farmers. However, all the men are away fighting and the only hope the women have is to band together under Hewitt and learn how to fight.

programme.title: Darkman
programme.desc: Comic-book-style tale of a hideously scarred scientist and his bid to exact revenge on the thugs who disfigured him. With the discovery of a revolutionary synthetic skin, Dr Peyton Westlake refashions himself as the shadowy avenger `Darkman', hiding away from his former life in an abandoned building and only taking to the streets in disguise.

programme.title: Gagarine
programme.desc: Yuri, 16, has lived all his life in Gagarin Towers, a vast red-brick housing project on the outskirts of Paris. He dreams of becoming an astronaut. When plans to demolish Gagarin Towers leak out, Yuri joins the resistance. With his friends Diana and Houssam, he embarks on a mission to save their home, which has become his "starship."

programme.desc: Adam must eat 12 of San Jose's hottest hot wings without taking a drink - for five minutes after the food is gone.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam must eat 17 overstuffed hot dogs in less than half an hour.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Florida, Adam visits an English-style seaside eatery and an Amish restaurant.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Durham, North Carolina, Adam competes in the Doughman, a charity relay race that gives a whole new meaning to `eat and run'.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In the Lone Star State, Adam enjoys some mouth-watering, Texas-sized grub. Can he put away a 48 ounce steak?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Denver, Adam tackles a giant breakfast burrito containing seven potatoes, a pound of ham and 12 eggs.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Arriving in Minneapolis, Adam attempts to eat a metre of bratwurst and sauerkraut.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Las Vegas, can Adam manage to put away a six pound, two foot long Burrito?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Charleston, South Carolina, Adam gets a big serving of Southern charm and an even bigger helping of low country cuisine.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Alaska Adam's belt-busting challenges include a seven-course meal that must be devoured in ninety minutes.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by top TV chef Monica Galetti. They're in Oxford, where Monica discovers the delights of an organic local distiller. James takes a river trip to a fantastic pick your own farm where he picks some raspberries and he uses them to cook a delicious pudding on the banks of the river. James also visits one of the world's best chefs, Raymond Blanc, at his restaurant Le Manoir aux Quat'Saisons, where he enjoys some of Raymond's Michelin-starred dishes.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is in South Wales, accompanied by Michelin-starred chef Stephen Terry. James discovers award-winning Welsh wine and cooks a Welsh lamb ragu on a beautiful mountainside. He samples the delights of organic Welsh chocolate and visits cooking legend Shaun Hill at his renowned restaurant The Walnut Tree. The day comes to an end at Barry Island, where James and Stephen enjoy all the fairground rides at this famous resort and James cooks Barry Island churros overlooking the beach.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's the semi-final, and the remaining bakers are tested on their patisserie skills, with a domed Signature dish, a towering Technical and a display case Showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The stakes couldn't be higher as Paul and Prue set three challenges that test every aspect of the bakers' skills. Who will be crowned 2019's Bake Off champion?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bake Off's back with a twist, as 12 new bakers form a bubble and enter the tent. It's Cake Week and the bakers tackle Battenberg, a turning-out technical and a heroic showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Biscuit Week and the bakers make a sophisticated fruit, nut and chocolate signature, produce two tropical technicals, and create an elaborate table setting showstopper.

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: In this episode, James visits a huge indoor food market in Bilbao, takes a funicular to a famous steak restaurant, and heads to a winery on a seabed - we also see how La Carolina is made as James cooks cod, squid ink arancini, Basque cheesecake and seafood on a boat.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James is bringing his culinary tour of the UK to his neck of the woods as he spends precious time in his adopted home county of Hampshire. He's taking his gun dog Cooper to a training session at Laverstoke before cooking an amazing seasonal dish of partridge with vegetables and a sloe gin sauce. James hangs out with his best friend Jody Scheckter, an ex-formula one racer with an epic car collection, and a surprising passion for buffalo mozzarella.

programme.title: River Cottage: Road Trip
programme.desc: Hugh heads to the Lake District and into Yorkshire in search of the finest northern tea experience, and snaffles a few good cake recipes to bring back down south. His trip almost over, he's heading back to Dorset, where a busy autumn at River Cottage HQ beckons.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Mary embraces the British countryside with fresh and honest bucolic cooking inspired by what is grown on farms and in gardens, from a joyous pasta inspired by a classic French herb sauce, to a perfect way to partner asparagus, and how to serve up the most scrumptious roasted vegetables. Mary makes her way through vegetable rows and orchards, ending with her own inspired version of the delicious tarte tatin.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Mary has always adored entertaining and pulls out some of her finest classics that will leave any guest begging for the next invitation, be it a romantic meal for two or hosting a big meal for friends and family. From her divine Dover sole cooked to perfection, a genius way to make beetroot gravadlax and a sticky chicken recipe that will have them knocking down the doors, to an ambitious crispy roast duck and a simple but exquisite raspberry mousse.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree's making mind-blowing sweet treats for her latest blog post. There's super s'mores, stunning marbled dipped apples, deep-fried cheesecake bites with strawberry sauce and amazing hard candy shot glasses topped up with fizz.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: While Ree's husband Ladd and his father Chuck dismantle a historic cattle feed storehouse, she cooks them a lunch of juicy steak and eggs benedict piled onto garlic butter English muffins and drizzled in a spicy hollandaise sauce.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Glazed Pork Fillet and Tapas Bruschetta
programme.desc: Jamie makes glazed pork fillet, cajun-style pepper rice and barbecue sauce, and tapas bruschetta, and golden grilled sardines.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Lamb Kofte and Keralan Veggie Curry
programme.desc: On the menu this time is lamb kofte, pitta and greek salad, and Keralan veggie curry, poppadoms, rice and minty yoghurt.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina Garten's close friends, director Rob Marshall and producer John DeLuca, visit her at home, where she makes them all refreshing Watermelon Cosmopolitans. Then John takes over in the kitchen to make Salmon Burgers with his mom's Simplest Slaw.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bake Off is back as 12 new bakers enter the iconic white tent. It's cake week, and the bakers tackle a striking sponge and a scaled-down version of a home close to their hearts.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Rachel shows how to make a rich poppy seed cake, smothered with a vanilla buttercream icing. She also meets Michelin-starred chef Atul Kochhar, renowned for his masterful use of spices, and he takes her on a culinary trip back to his childhood, baking some sweet treats his mum will be proud of.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Continuing his British Adventure, James travels to Cornwall, where his travelling companion is local hero, chef Paul Ainsworth. James starts with a trip to Camel Valley Vineyard and cooks chicken Veronique amongst the vines. Paul hops across the estuary to taste the award-winning oysters produced by Porthilly Farm, a five-generation family-run dairy farm who diversified into oysters, and James uses some in his individual beef pies with oysters.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: Among the desserts created are the classic baked Alaska, pavlova and some macaroons.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is finals week and 24 of the nation's finest chefs have been whittled down to just eight. Over the course of four hour-long shows, they will battle it out in the kitchen for the chance to get a dish to the Comic Relief Banquet. Actress Patricia Hodge, one of the stars of sitcom `Miranda', joins judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort to taste all eight winning regional starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their fish courses. They are all vying to finish in the top three and still be in with a chance of cooking at the special banquet. Tensions are high but there are still plenty of laughs as new dishes are introduced under competition rules, including a Sicilian influenced dish called the `Cod Father'. Impressionist Ronni Ancona joins the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Comedian Ade Edmondson tucks into the chefs' main courses to help decide which three will remain in contention to cook at the special banquet. However, the chefs are in for a major surprise as the judges deliver unexpected news about the day's competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's judgement day as the chefs not only learn the top three desserts but the judges announce the winning menu. `Fast Show' writer and performer Charlie Higson helps judge the puddings. Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort decide on the four winning dishes, but there is a problem - they are torn between two dishes for one of the courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the climax of the competition as the winning chefs cook at the Comic Relief Banquet at the Royal Albert Hall. Special guest and co-founder of the charity Richard Curtis welcomes guests - the unsung heroes of charity work, as well as those who have benefited from their help and some of the celebrities who have inspired us to do something funny for money.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline O'Donnell and Neil Rankin go head to head to represent Scotland. Today they must impress the mystery veteran judge with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs, double Michelin starred Stevie McLaughlin, experienced Jacqueline O'Donnell and newcomer Neil Rankin fight it out for the fish course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the main course for Scottish chefs Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline O'Donnell and Neil Rankin. With the scores close, the pressure is on for all three and they're each hoping to impress with unique and individual dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course and the Scottish chefs' fates hang in the balance as at the end of today, one of them must be sent home. For Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline O'Donnell and Neil Rankin there's no room for error.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs fight it out for just one place in the finals, cooking all four of their dishes again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Also scoring the menus is guest judge and guest of honour Jim Radford.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the South West's finest chefs, returning contender Emily Watkins, experienced Dominic Chapman and newcomer Josh Eggleton fight it out. Today they must cook their starter courses to be judged by the surprise veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs, returner Emily Watkins and Michelin starred Dominic Chapman and Josh Eggleton do battle for the fish course. Dom has been inspired by his research trip to the scene of the D Day landings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs Emily Watkins, Dominic Chapman and Josh Eggleton cook their main courses for the veteran judge. With the competition so tight can one of them pull out in front before the deciding dessert course?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course and the South West chefs' last chance to secure a place in the regional final. Two will make it through to cook their menus for the judges but either Emily Watkins, Dominic Chapman or Josh Eggleton must leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two South West chefs left standing cook their four courses again for the judges. They're joined by special guest judge and hero of the D-Day landings, 93-year-old war veteran Ken Sturdy, a Royal Navy signalman in World War II.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Wales' top chefs, returning contender Mary Ann Gilchrist, former military man David Kelman and fellow newcomer Andy Beaumont compete for the last remaining place in the finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs Andy Beaumont, David Kelman and Mary Ann Gilchrist are all hoping to score highly with their very individual main courses. Mary Ann's taking a risk with another austerity themed dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course and the Welsh chefs' last chance to impress. After David Kelman's main course result, Mary Ann Gilchrist and Andy Beaumont must do everything they can not to crack under the pressure.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Welsh chefs fight it out for the last place in the finals. They must both cook all four courses of their D Day inspired menus again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge Martin Bell.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in London's Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barber's in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: A daily delivery of bespoke Mindfulness content designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Brand-new series and Bradley and Barney's Aussie adventure begins in the iconic city of Sydney, where they take in the sights of the famous Sydney Harbour Bridge and Opera House with a terrifying aerobatic flight that takes its toll on Brad. The pair end their day with a treat as Barney has arranged an exclusive and unique dinner. The next day the boys head out of the city towards the beautiful Blue Mountains, famed for its spectacular scenery.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney are well into their Australian road trip, and before they leave the beautiful Blue Mountains, Barney has an extreme challenge planned for his dad as they must traverse a ravine on a rope! The boys then head into the countryside and make a stop in the land of sheep, where they learn the art of shearing. Their road trip then takes them to a tiny town where they join the local community who've organised a mighty challenge that involves pulling a gigantic truck.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Laura Woods presents live World Cup coverage as Australia and Turkey get their campaigns underway in Vancouver. With Group D also featuring USA and Paraguay, both sides know a winning start could prove crucial in one of the tournament's most competitive groups. Analysis comes from former Socceroos manager Ange Postecoglou and Spain World Cup winner Juan Mata, with commentary from Joe Speight and Lucy Ward.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James visits Languedoc, famous for its wine and the hearty meaty dish, cassoulet. He turns up the heat with a steak cooked on a barbecue in the blazing sunshine.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region and pays a visit to the de La Rochefoucauld family.

programme.title: Ainsley's Fantastic Flavours
programme.desc: With bright colours, big tastes and dishes that add a little sunshine to every bite, Ainsley brings sunny vibes straight to your table. From Greek chicken gyros to mozzarella panzanella with a fruity twist, these recipes are perfect for those long, lazy days and holiday get-togethers! Lisa Snowdon pops in to make her summertime favourite seafood linguine, and chef Joseph Denison Carey goes strawberry picking in the sun.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan this time are actress Barbara Flynn and The Repair Shop favourites Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch, who are better known as the Teddy Bear Ladies - Alan also gets a masterclass in milking goats, surveys some vintage bicycles, and samples the best summer red wines.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: rue is joined in her kitchen by Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge as he cooks smoky beef and short ribs which can also be cooked on a barbecue - Tom also talks about when he first started cooking as a boy, and then has an accident while making a sauce with a food mixer that leaves him covered in food! Elsewhere, John heads off to Larkhill Vineyard to pick up some tips on becoming a successful wine producer while Prue has a mouthwatering hack for garlic confit which can be stored in a jar.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: From Russia with Love
programme.desc: James Bond finds his life under threat when he aids a beautiful Russian woman who wants to defect to the West with a politically invaluable cipher machine. It soon becomes apparent that the spy is merely being used as a pawn by SPECTRE, a group of international criminals intent on finishing off 007 once and for all.

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Another thrilling episode of the quiz in which three celebrities take on an extraordinary machine in the hope of winning thousands of pounds for charity - this time sees host Ben Shephard welcome McFly's Harry Judd, The Great British Sewing Bee's Patrick Grant, and comedian Angela Barnes as they take on the machine for its jackpot and the last celebrity standing will facing a 20,000-pound dilemma.

programme.title: The Chase: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts a celebrity edition of the hit quiz show. Four well-known celebrities have to work together to try and win thousands of pounds for charity.

programme.title: ITV Evening News
programme.desc: The latest headlines from around the world. Followed by a national weather forecast.

programme.title: ITV News London
programme.desc: The latest news in London and the South East, plus local weather.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Live coverage of the 2026 FIFA World Cup match between four-time winners Germany and Curaçao, the Caribbean island nation playing their first-ever World Cup match. Mark Pougatch is joined in the New York studio by Emma Hayes, Juan Mata and Ian Wright, with commentary in the Houston Stadium from Seb Hutchinson and Andros Townsend.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: The Netherlands and Japan open their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against each other in Dallas. It's the first game in Group F which also features Sweden and Tunisia. Laura Woods presents with Roy Keane, Gary Neville and Ange Postecoglou providing studio analysis, commentary from Jon Champion and Ally McCoist, with Adam Richman providing an alternative view of the World Cup.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathan's Parents' Evening
programme.desc: Joel Dommett and his mum Penny, Tony Bellew and his son Corey, and Vicky Pattison and her mum Carroll compete in the comedy game show where success lies in the hands of the parents - as Joel tries to overcome his second-place curse and Tony and Corey's bickering threatens to get the better of them, Vicky's past party lifestyle might just lead her and her mum to victory in a show with plenty of surprises that include Romesh's mum's method for getting away with murder.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Semra Hunter presents live coverage as Sweden and Tunisia play their first match of the FIFA World Cup in Monterrey. Group F also features Belgium and South Korea. Analysis from former Lioness Karen Carney and ex-Jamaica international Jobi McAnuff, with commentary from Joe Speight and Lucy Ward.

programme.desc: Ian St John and Ron Yeats reflect on their time at Liverpool with the great Bill Shankly.

programme.title: Retro Reds

programme.title: One Week In May: The Story Of A Unique Treble
programme.desc: A look at the unforgettable 2000/01 season when Gerard Houllier's team charged to three trophies and Champions League qualification for the first time.

programme.title: McAteer: Through the Storm
programme.desc: Jason McAteer opens up about his battle with depression and examines the wider issue of mental health in football.

programme.title: Ee-Aye-Addio: 1965 and All That

programme.title: Bugsy Moran
programme.desc: A documentary looking back on the illustrious Anfield career of Bootroom legend Ronnie Moran.

programme.title: Macca and Macca

programme.title: Sixty Minutes With...

programme.title: 130 Greatest LFC Goals
programme.desc: The complete countdown, from 130 to number 1, of Liverpool's most memorable goals; be it in terms of importance, iconic status or simply sheer quality.

programme.title: The Match

programme.title: Premier League Rewind
programme.desc: Archive Premier League action from Roy Evans' time as manager. This hour features games against Crystall Palace (1994) Nottingham Forest (1996), Aston Villa (1996) and Barnsley (1998).

programme.title: Press Box
programme.desc: Media pundits debate the topical issues surrounding Liverpool Football Club and football in general.

programme.title: Goal of the Season

programme.title: Retro Review Show

programme.title: Goal Rush

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition takes place in and around Leeds, where the first to host is 24-year-old digital account manager Alice. Alice has never held a dinner party before, but she's hoping her family favourites will hit the spot and win her the grand. She's hoping to impress Danielle, Andy, Lynne and Matt. With gob-smacking revelations, cringe-worthy party tricks and a huge Pavlova, can Alice walk away with the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's night two of the competition in Leeds, and company director Andy is hoping to win the grand with a menu of fancy restaurant inspired fare. But Alice and Danielle are more impressed with Andy's Ferrari than his food. And Lynne is more concerned with turning out a perfect Mojito in the cocktail making challenge than anything else. Will Andy's posh nosh be to everyone's taste, and can he hold his composure till the end of the night to bag top spot?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the third night of the competition in Leeds, and fun-loving student Danielle is hoping her hearty and high calorie menu will bag her the prize. The laughs come thick and fast for sports car fanatic Andy, while account manager Alice finds herself in food heaven. But when Danielle serves dessert in her wedding dress, lighting technician Matt is prompted to propose to sales consultant Lynne and fails spectacularly.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth host in Leeds this week is theatre technician Matt, who's planning to scoop the prize with a menu of tried and tested family favourites. The food is a winner for student Danielle and she's even more impressed by his juggling skills. But neither hit the mark for IT director Andy, who declares it the worst night of the week so far and things go from bad to worse for Matt when Alice's dessert ends up on the floor.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition in Leeds, and jeweller Lynne is hoping her night will sparkle with a menu of made of her own creations. While talk at the table turns to plastic surgery and Botox, Andy and Danielle aren't too impressed with Lynne's food. Can she rescue her dinner party with a memorable karaoke performance and sing her way to top spot and bag the thousand pounds?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Frances dreams of buying a holiday home that she can enjoy with her friends and family in Spain's beautiful coastal town of Nerja. Danni Menzies helps her find the perfect place for her budget of £200k.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Neil are starting a new life in Lecce in southern Italy, and want a property that's close to the beach with a guest annexe. Jasmine Harman shows them what their £200k budget can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gilly and Andy, who got engaged in Portugal 26 years ago, have decided that the Silver Coast is the perfect place to spend their retirement. Can Ben Hillman find their dream home for £210k?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George is back helping more people make their period homes fit for modern life. In Manchester, an Edwardian home is dragged kicking and screaming into the modern day.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the rural outskirts of Leeds, where Karl loves the idyllic location, but Paul finds their home too isolated and wants to move.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Beverly and her mum Margaret are looking for a rural house with space for family visits in Umbria in Italy, for Beverly's budget of £150,000. Sara Damergi shows them five great options.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Rachel and John can't wait to buy a holiday home for all the family in the town of Oliva on the stunning Valencian coastline. Ben Hillman shows them what their £100k can buy.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This week's competition kicks off at The Red Lion in Charing Heath, Kent. Hosts Lee and Claire hope their matey approach to hosting will set them apart, and treat the B&Bers to a masterclass in hospitality at nearby Leeds Castle.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B battle continues in Battle in East Sussex, at Dean and Becky's Glamp in Style campsite. The hosts certainly have novelty on their side, with their unique accommodation. But not everyone is keen to embrace the alternative glamping experience.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Helen and Phil host the week's third visit at Tottington Manor in West Sussex. Some guests are bowled over by the décor, but others raise their eyebrows at the high prices. An afternoon at a rum distillery makes Helen and Phil the toast of the town.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sam and Claudia host the week's final visit at their self-catering Lakeview Studios in Dorset. One couple find a problem in the toilet, before battle lines are drawn at a tank museum.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day promises to be a fiery affair after two sets of B&Bers in particular participated in tit-for-tat marking throughout the week. Then the payments are revealed and the winners announced.

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Time is critical as Dr Scott Taylor's patient deteriorates with a brain bleed. Dr Tristan Harding must straighten a young man's dislocated ankle before the blood supply cuts off.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: James is rushed to St George's after a head-on car crash. Margaret's had a fall, but her daughter has tales to tell of her mother's adventurous life in London in the swinging 60s.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A train driver is rushed to St George's after colliding with a car. In addition, a gold-medal-winning 13-year-old BMX rider has a suspected pelvis injury after crashing at the race track.

programme.title: Teen Mom OG
programme.desc: For four seasons fans of `Teen Mom' watched Farrah, Maci, Amber and Catelynn face multiple challenges in their first years of motherhood, including such things as graduating from high school, relationships, getting jobs, beginning college and moving out on their own. Oh, and raising their first child. Now the original girls - hence, the `OG' in the revived series title - return to reveal the challenges of raising children on camera, and how life in the public eye has shaped them as parents and people. It's an unprecedented view of young mothers facing the responsibilities of parenthood as their children enter kindergarten, start little league and reach other milestones of youth.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2
programme.desc: Kail uncovers a secret Vee & Javi have been keeping. Leah introduces Jaylan to the girls. Jade tries to create stability for Kloie, who is struggling in Sean's absence.

programme.title: Teen Mom OG
programme.desc: For four seasons fans of `Teen Mom' watched Farrah, Maci, Amber and Catelynn face multiple challenges in their first years of motherhood, including such things as graduating from high school, relationships, getting jobs, beginning college and moving out on their own. Oh, and raising their first child. Now the original girls - hence, the `OG' in the revived series title - return to reveal the challenges of raising children on camera, and how life in the public eye has shaped them as parents and people. It's an unprecedented view of young mothers facing the responsibilities of parenthood as their children enter kindergarten, start little league and reach other milestones of youth.

programme.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team
programme.desc: There is excitement in the air as 600 hopefuls arrive for the preliminary auditions. Each contestant has less than two minutes to perform a freestyle dance routine in front of the judges. Only 115 dancers will reach the semi-finals, and only 75 will be called back to the final auditions for a chance to compete against the returning veterans.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are in Granny and Grandpa Pig's Garden. Granny's friends are playing folk music! They play music for Grandpa Pig and then Grandpa Pig makes apple juice for everyone!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It is bedtime and Peppa calls Kylie Kangaroo and is surprised to learn that it is not morning or winter where she is! Daddy Pig explains this using a big ball to represent the Earth.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While visiting Suzy and Emily's house, Peppa is surprised that there are extra rules which she does not have in her own house. On the drive home, Peppa explains to Mummy Pig that different houses have different rules.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: At the National Railway Museum, Alex and Peter help get the most famous locomotive in the world, the Flying Scotsman, into steam. The team take a ride of a lifetime as the loco travels along its original route, connecting the two most important financial capitals of the Empire, London and Edinburgh, and Alex finds out what it is like for catering staff with 250 hungry mouths to feed.

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team head to the South Devon Railway to explore the life of the branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Ruth hand-milks a local breed of cow, and discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle. After undergoing an eyesight test, Victorian-style; Peter joins the footplate crew on the South Devon line. But it is not all plain-sailing when it comes to driving the milk train through the night.

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team find out how the combination of increased leisure time and affordable rail transport brought a new kind of freedom for working-class Victorians. Ruth travels along the beautiful south Devon coast from Paignton to Kingswear where she helps get a paddle steamer prepared for a journey up the River Dart.

programme.title: Constantine the Great
programme.desc: A Game of Thrones: Ancient history knows few rulers like Constantine the Great. Part one follows archaeologists on the stages of his life from Naissus to Rome. (Part 1 of 2, Ep1)

programme.title: Constantine the Great
programme.desc: A New Rome: Constantine's claim to power knows no bounds. Part two follows the emperor's archaeological traces to the new Rome, Constantinople. (Part 2 of 2, Ep2)

programme.desc: The Sound Of Change was a benefit concert held at Twickenham Stadium, London, England, as part of the Chime For Change initiative, founded by Beyoncé with the help of actress Salma Hayek and Gucci's creative director, Frida Giannini.

programme.title: Rihanna - Live at Made in America
programme.desc: Rihanna performs on the first day of the Made in America Festival in 2016. Rihanna was the headliner of the festival, sampling songs from her album Anti as well as her older hits.

programme.title: Public Enemy: Live at The Metro Theatre
programme.desc: Public Enemy, also known as PE, is an influential hip hop group from Long Island, New York, known for its politically charged lyrics, criticism of the media, and active interest in the concerns of the African American community. In 2004, Rolling Stone Magazine ranked Public Enemy number forty-four on its list of the Immortals: 100. Here, the group performs at The Metro Theatre, in Sydney, Australia, on December 27, 2008.

programme.title: Duane Allman - Song of the South
programme.desc: Duane Allman, the lead guitarist and driving force behind Southern Rock pioneers The Allman Brothers Band, lived a tragically short life, but during his brief career he changed music forever.

programme.title: The Killers - Live at Rock Am Ring
programme.desc: The Killers perform at Rock Am Ring, a music festival that takes place annually at the Nürburgring Race Track in Western Germany, with tracks like When You Were Young and The World We Live In.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search of automotive bargains. programme.title: The First Wives Club
programme.desc: When Brenda Cushman, Elise Elliot and Annie Paradis are reunited at the funeral of a college friend, they discover they have another thing in common: they've all been dumped by their husbands, Morty, Bill and Aaron, for a younger model. Vowing revenge, they decide to hit them where it hurts most: the pocket. Using help and their various skills, they begin to pick them off one by on.

programme.title: Dumb and Dumberer: When Harry Met Lloyd
programme.desc: A grant-greedy principal puts two clueless teens in a bogus class for students with special needs.

programme.title: Police Academy
programme.desc: Misfits realise their dreams of becoming cops when a mayoral decree forces the police academy to open its doors to all.

programme.title: Just Go With It
programme.desc: His heart recently broken, plastic surgeon Danny Maccabee (Adam Sandler) pretends to be married so he can enjoy future dates with no strings attached. His web of lies works, but when he meets Palmer (Brooklyn Decker) -- the gal of his dreams -- she resists involvement. Instead of coming clean, Danny enlists Katherine (Jennifer Aniston), his assistant, to pose as his soon-to-be-ex-wife. Instead of solving Danny's problems, the lies create more trouble.

programme.title: Instant Family
programme.desc: When Pete and Ellie decide to start a family, they stumble into the world of foster care adoption. They hope to take in one small child, but when they meet three siblings, including a rebellious 15-year-old girl, they find themselves speeding from zero to three kids overnight. Now, Pete and Ellie must try to learn the ropes of instant parenthood in the hope of becoming a family.

programme.title: Little Fockers
programme.desc: Greg and Pam have been together for ten years, and now they're the proud parents of two adorable twins. However, money is tight and in order to keep his family afloat, devoted nurse Greg takes a second job working for a drug company. That development doesn't sit well with Jack, whose previous suspicions regarding his hapless son-in-law soon come back in full force after learning of this development.

programme.title: The Wedding Singer
programme.desc: When one-time rock hopeful Robbie is jilted at the altar, he vents his bitterness in song, even when singing at other people's weddings. Only true love can redeem the broken hearted songster but fortunately, he's about to meet a woman who will spin him right round (like a record).

programme.title: Mean Girls
programme.desc: After 15 years in African bush country, Cady knows all about herd instincts, but nothing has prepared her for high school. She joins a popular but cruelly manipulative crowd of girls known as the `Plastics' and secretly mocks them, but the fun stops when one betrays her and steals the boy she fancies.

programme.title: Risky Business
programme.desc: A normally well-behaved teenager goes wild while his parents are out of town. In charge at his parents' smart house, the 17-year-old sees the chance to make a fast buck when the prostitute, on the run from a scary pimp, appears at his door. He takes her in, and, following his school's entrepreneurial teachings, lets her turn the house into a brothel.

programme.title: Blades of Glory
programme.desc: Chazz Michael Michaels and Jimmy MacElroy are the two titans of skating. At the Winter Olympics, the fierce rivals find themselves forced to share the winner's podium when they both score gold. The shower of glory doesn't last for long, as the pair descend into fisticuffs and set the mascot on fire. Disgraced and stripped of their medals, they can get back on ice, but only if they dance with each other as a pair.

programme.title: Gremlins 2: The New Batch
programme.desc: Six years after defeating the Gremlins, Billy and Kate have grown up and moved to New York. However, Billy stumbles across Gizmo in a genetics lab and when he gets wet, all hell breaks loose, and the stage is set for more deliciously bad taste humour.

programme.title: Gremlins 2: The New Batch
programme.desc: Six years after defeating the Gremlins, Billy and Kate have grown up and moved to New York. However, Billy stumbles across Gizmo in a genetics lab and when he gets wet, all hell breaks loose, and the stage is set for more deliciously bad taste humour.

programme.title: The Room Next Door
programme.desc: Ingrid and Martha became close friends while working together at the same magazine. After years of being out of touch, they meet again in an extreme but strangely sweet situation.

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview
programme.desc: Previews of the great films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights showing on Sky.

programme.title: Cicada
programme.desc: Ben, a young bisexual man, comes out to the world and develops an intense relationship with Sam, a closeted man struggling with deep wounds of his own. As summer progresses and their intimacy grows, secrets from the past come to light.

programme.title: Miss You Already
programme.desc: A woman undergoes treatment to battle breast cancer while her best friend tries to get pregnant.

programme.title: Nuremberg
programme.desc: As the Nuremberg trials are set to begin, a U.S. Army psychiatrist gets locked in a dramatic psychological showdown with accused Nazi war criminal Hermann Göring.

programme.title: Moneyball
programme.desc: Faced with a tight budget, a baseball manager and his partner recruit bargain bin players that scouts have labelled as flawed, but have game-winning potential.

programme.title: Terms of Endearment
programme.desc: While widow Aurora fends off a stream of admirers, daughter Emma learns the hard facts of married life.

programme.title: Lost in Translation
programme.desc: In Tokyo shooting a whisky commercial, middle-aged actor Bob Harris becomes attracted to Charlotte, the young wife of a celebrity photographer. Together they ride out their boredom of hotel life and their bewilderment of the city.

programme.title: A Star Is Born
programme.desc: Hard-drinking country music star Jackson Maine discovers, and falls in love with, a struggling but talented singer named Ally. As her career quickly takes off, Jackson starts to realize that his best days may be behind him.

programme.title: Chicago
programme.desc: Roxy Hart is sent to prison for shooting her lying lover. In prison she meets tabloid darling and headline singer, Velma Kelly, who is also in for a double murder. With the help of a slick Chicago lawyer, she learns to play the media game and vies for the valuable press attention that will get her out of jail.

programme.title: Miss You Already
programme.desc: A woman undergoes treatment to battle breast cancer while her best friend tries to get pregnant.

programme.title: Cicada
programme.desc: Ben, a young bisexual man, comes out to the world and develops an intense relationship with Sam, a closeted man struggling with deep wounds of his own. As summer progresses and their intimacy grows, secrets from the past come to light.

programme.title: Cicada
programme.desc: Ben, a young bisexual man, comes out to the world and develops an intense relationship with Sam, a closeted man struggling with deep wounds of his own. As summer progresses and their intimacy grows, secrets from the past come to light.

programme.title: Boy Meets Boy
programme.desc: Harry has been partying for 48 hours when he meets Johannes on the dance floor of a club in Berlin. With 15 hours until his flight home, Johannes offers to help him print his boarding pass.

programme.title: The Wiz
programme.desc: In this version of the classic story - based on the smash Broadway musical - Dorothy is a shy 24-year-old Harlem kindergarten teacher who is brought by a snowstorm to the mysterious Land of Oz, a sprawling, decaying, nightmarish, and dangerous megalopolis that resembles the physical characteristics of New York City.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: Transformers One
programme.desc: Brothers-in-arms Orion Pax and D-16 become sworn enemies Optimus Prime and Megatron.

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2
programme.desc: Reformed criminals Mr. Wolf, Mr. Snake, Mr. Piranha, Mr. Shark and Ms. Tarantula find themselves hijacked into a high-stakes heist that's masterminded by a new team of delinquents they never saw coming -- the Bad Girls.

programme.title: Ghostbusters: Afterlife
programme.desc: When a single mother and her two children move to a new town, they soon discover that they have a connection to the original Ghostbusters and their grandfather's secret legacy.

programme.title: Paddington
programme.desc: A young Peruvian bear with a passion for all things British travels to London in search of a home. Finding himself lost and alone at Paddington Station, he begins to realise that city life is not all he had imagined - until he meets the kindly Brown family, who offer him a temporary haven.

programme.title: Paddington 2
programme.desc: Settled in with the Brown family, Paddington the bear is a popular member of the community who spreads joy and marmalade wherever he goes. One fine day, he spots a pop-up book in an antique shop: the perfect present for his beloved aunt's 100th birthday. When a thief steals the prized book, Paddington embarks on an epic quest to unmask the culprit before Aunt Lucy's big celebration.

programme.title: Paddington in Peru
programme.desc: Paddington and his adopted family, the Browns, decide to visit his aunt Lucy in Peru, but circumstances lead them towards misadventures in the Amazon rainforest and mountains of Peru.

programme.title: Pokémon Detective Pikachu
programme.desc: A young man joins forces with Detective Pikachu to unravel the mystery behind his father's disappearance. Chasing clues through the streets of Ryme City, the dynamic duo soon discover a devious plot that poses a threat to the Pokémon universe.

programme.title: Cats & Dogs
programme.desc: Unknown to humans, all-out war between cats and dogs has raged since the beginning of time. Now the cats face defeat if scientist Professor Brody can perfect a formula that will make all humans non-allergic to dogs. The cats' leader, Mr Tinkles, plans to steal the serum, but first he must get past fearless dog agent Lou who, thanks to a mistake in recruitment, guards the Brody family.

programme.title: Monster Trucks
programme.desc: A high school senior befriends a strange subterranean creature after building a monster truck from bits and pieces of scrapped cars.

programme.title: Daddy Day Care
programme.desc: Two fathers get laid off from their well-paid jobs and are forced to stay at home to look after their kids. Seeing the opportunity to make money, the two of them open their own day-care centre. With very little experience and some fairly unconventional ideas on child care, can they really hope to compete against the prim and proper Chapman Academy, which has monopolised the child care industry in their town?

programme.title: Paulie
programme.desc: A wisecracking parrot caged in a research lab tells his life story to a lonely janitor. Before coming to the science lab, he has passed through the hands of a succession of owners, from a petty criminal to a man who runs a parrot-dancing act. Touched by his colourful story, the janitor conspires to liberate the talking bird.

programme.title: Free Willy
programme.desc: A heart-warming tale about a troubled young boy and his friendship with a captive orca whale named Willy. On bad terms with his new foster parents and set to work in an aquarium under court order, the boy turns to the whale for companionship. When he uncovers a cruel plot by its owner to kill the whale in order to claim a big insurance payout, the lad becomes determined to save Willy's life and release him back into the wild.

programme.title: Free Willy
programme.desc: A heart-warming tale about a troubled young boy and his friendship with a captive orca whale named Willy. On bad terms with his new foster parents and set to work in an aquarium under court order, the boy turns to the whale for companionship. When he uncovers a cruel plot by its owner to kill the whale in order to claim a big insurance payout, the lad becomes determined to save Willy's life and release him back into the wild.

programme.title: Buffalo Kids
programme.desc: Arriving in New York City in 1886, two orphaned Irish siblings embark on a wild cross-country journey aboard a transcontinental train. They make a new friend and encounter heroes and villains in their quest to find a new home.

programme.title: Tootsie
programme.desc: An out-of-work actor dresses in drag for an audition, only to land the female lead in a TV soap. When he becomes the most popular character on the show, he feels compelled to carry the persona into his everyday life. While bringing him phenomenal success as an `actress', life as a woman also has its problems - especially when he falls for an actress with whom he works.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: An alcoholic millionaire is threatened with losing his inheritance if he does not marry the girl of his parents' choosing. Despite his dislike for his prospective bride, he bows to the inevitable only to meet a light-fingered waitress who steals his heart. John Gielgud won an Oscar for his role as Arthur's loyal but outspoken valet.

programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil
programme.desc: Lighthearted adventures of a pair of news vendors in New York - one deaf, one blind - who go on the run when they are blamed for the murder of a customer. Each has seen - or heard - just a whiff of a clue, and a frantic chase ensues as the duo struggle to clear their names.

programme.title: Tootsie
programme.desc: An out-of-work actor dresses in drag for an audition, only to land the female lead in a TV soap. When he becomes the most popular character on the show, he feels compelled to carry the persona into his everyday life. While bringing him phenomenal success as an `actress', life as a woman also has its problems - especially when he falls for an actress with whom he works.

programme.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles
programme.desc: Fastidious businessman Neal Page and slobbish shower-curtain salesman Del Griffith are the reluctant odd couple trying to get home from New York to Chicago to be with their families at Thanksgiving whose thwarted travel plans keep throwing them together in.

programme.title: Caddyshack
programme.desc: A rich property developer wants to take over an exclusive country club and so challenges its pompous president to a game of golf leading to utter mayhem, and a stream of surreal and crude visual gags.

programme.title: Singin' in the Rain
programme.desc: When the transition is being made from silent films to `talkies', everyone has trouble adapting. Don and Lina have been cast repeatedly as a romantic couple, but when their latest film is remade into a musical, only Don has the voice for the new singing part. After a lot of practise with a diction coach, Lina still sounds terrible, and Kathy, a bright young aspiring actress, is hired to record over her voice.

programme.title: Blazing Saddles
programme.desc: Irreverent spoof about a black sheriff hired by a crooked speculator to drive citizens out of an all-white town which lies in the path of a railroad. The townspeople turn on the inexperienced sheriff, and he is forced to turn to a convict, once the fastest gun in the West, for help.

programme.title: The Man With Two Brains
programme.desc: Zany horror spoof about a brilliant brain surgeon who falls in love with a female brain stored in a jar. The brain is part of an impressive collection held by a colleague in Austria and, upon learning of his wife's infidelity, the surgeon allows himself to be charmed. Communicating telepathically with one of the specimens in the laboratory, he falls in love - but needs a body to put it in.

programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil

programme.title: Annie
programme.desc: Daddy Warbucks opens his estate to a spunky orphan in this adaptation of the popular comic strip and hit Broadway show.

programme.title: The Big Chill
programme.desc: Reunited at a funeral, several college friends look back at how their lives have changed since the turbulent 1960s.

programme.title: About Last Night ...
programme.desc: Two Chicago singles try to build a more meaningful relationship with each other following a one-night stand.

programme.title: Fools Rush In
programme.desc: After a one-night stand, a couple decide to get married. However, it turns out to be a rocky road when both parties have to compromise their own cultural traditions.

programme.title: The Internship
programme.desc: A highly trained assassin is ready to dismantle the top-secret CIA programme that stole her youth. Assembling fellow operatives, she leads a bloody uprising that forces the CIA to unleash equally lethal forces to stop them.

programme.title: Tafiti: Across the Desert
programme.desc: When his grandfather gets bitten by a snake, a young meerkat embarks on an adventure across the desert to find the magical flower that can cure him.

programme.title: No Ordinary Heist
programme.desc: In heavily policed, post conflict Belfast, two Northern Bank workers become pawns in the largest cash heist in UK history, stealing millions to save their families' lives, without the thieves ever setting foot inside the bank.

programme.title: The Choral
programme.desc: As World War I rages on, Dr. Henry Guthrie takes over a British choral society that's lost most of its men to the army. The community soon discovers that the best response to the chaos of war is to make beautiful music together.

programme.title: They Will Kill You
programme.desc: A woman answers a cryptic ad for a housekeeping job at a luxurious yet foreboding New York City high-rise. Upon arrival, she discovers residents have vanished without a trace for decades, fuelling whispers of a satanic cult lurking in the shadows.

programme.desc: London undergoes a citywide evacuation when a World War II bomb is unearthed at a construction site. Amid the escalating chaos, a daring criminal operation is set in motion -- one that uses the evacuation as a cover for a meticulously planned heist.

programme.desc: On Feb. 8, 1977, disgruntled real-estate developer Tony Kiritsis takes mortgage broker Richard Hall hostage, keeping him tied up in his apartment with a sawn-off shotgun pointed at his chest.

programme.title: One Last Deal
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent races to secure a final lucrative contract for his star client, as both their careers and lives hang in the balance. Starring Danny Dyer.

programme.desc: Quarterback Cameron Cade trains at an isolated compound with his hero, Isaiah White. As training progresses, Isaiah's charisma turns into something darker, sending Cam down a disorienting rabbit hole that may cost him more than he ever bargained for.

programme.title: The Parenting
programme.desc: Boyfriends Josh and Rohan plan a weekend getaway to introduce their parents, only to discover that their rental is home to a 400-year-old poltergeist.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Friendship's Death
programme.desc: A British reporter meets female robot Friendship, who has been sent from another planet to war-torn 1970 Jordan.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Companion
programme.desc: A weekend getaway turns bloody and violent when a subservient android that's built for human companionship goes haywire.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Aeon Flux
programme.desc: Aeon lives in a world restricted to the last city on Earth, after a plague has destroyed the rest of the world. The city is ruled by a council and its chairman, and people are mysteriously disappearing. Aeon is an assassin for an underground group of rebels called the Monicans, and is assigned to remove the Chairman, but at the last minute she finds herself unable to do it.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Paycheck
programme.desc: An engineer who agreed to have his memory erased following a huge top-secret job discovers that he refused the massive paycheck offered to him in return for his work, keeping instead a small bag of worthless items. He uses these clues to piece together the true nature of his mysterious work, as the FBI, a mysterious woman, and a group of killers attempt to track him down.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: After Earth
programme.desc: When his father is injured, a youth traverses hostile terrain to recover a rescue beacon.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Flash Gordon
programme.desc: Flash Gordon travels to Mongo in his efforts to save Earth from Ming the Merciless. Along the way, he must battle the evil henchmen and strike up some allies if he is going to save the world from destruction.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: The Shining
programme.desc: Frustrated writer Jack Torrance takes a job as the winter caretaker at the ominous, mountain-locked Overlook Hotel so that he can write in peace. When he arrives there with his wife and son, they learn that the previous caretaker had gone mad. Slowly Jack becomes possessed by the evil, demonic presence in the hotel.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: The Craft
programme.desc: A new girl at an LA high school finds it difficult to make friends, and teams up with three fellow outcasts known as the Bitches of Eastwick. However, her telekinetic powers turn the supernatural games in which the three have been dabbling, into something much more sinister.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: The Crazies
programme.desc: David Dutton is the sheriff of picture-perfect American town Ogden Marsh. One night and out of the blue one of its citizens shows up during a baseball game with a shot-gun and ready to kill. This is the beginning of a spiral of violence and mayhem.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A deep space transporter crash-lands on a desolate planet circled by three suns. Among the survivors are pilot Carolyn Fry, mystic Abu `Imam' al-Walid, cop William J Johns and convicted criminal Richard B Riddick, and their first thoughts are of survival and escape. Then Fry finds out that the planet suffers a total eclipse every 22 years, which is when the hibernating, flesh-eating inhabitants awake and hunt.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: 47 Meters Down
programme.desc: Two sisters vacationing in Mexico become trapped in a shark cage on the ocean floor. As their oxygen starts to run out and with great white sharks circling them, the sisters must find a way to get to the surface alive.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Smile 2
programme.desc: Global pop sensation Skye Riley begins to experience increasingly terrifying and inexplicable events. Overwhelmed by the escalating horrors and pressures of fame, she must face her dark past to regain control of her life.

programme.title: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: A lieutenant general and a colonel face political opposition after ordering a drone missile strike to take out a group of suicide bombers in Nairobi, Kenya.

programme.title: Maggie Moore(s)
programme.desc: Police Chief Sanders unravels a web of small-town lies while investigating the bizarre murders of two women with the same name. He meets and quickly falls for Rita, a nosy neighbour who's eager to help solve the mystery.

programme.title: The Surfer
programme.desc: A man revisits his childhood beach to surf with his son. Humiliated by locals, he becomes entangled in an escalating conflict that pushes him to his limits.

programme.title: Black Bag
programme.desc: When his beloved wife, Kathryn, is suspected of betraying the nation, intelligence agent George Woodhouse faces the ultimate test -- loyalty to his marriage or his country.

programme.title: Juror #2
programme.desc: A juror for a high-profile murder trial finds himself struggling with a serious moral dilemma that could influence the verdict and potentially convict, or free, the accused killer.

programme.title: Captain Phillips
programme.desc: In 2009, Somali pirates storm a U.S. containership and hold Capt. Richard Phillips and his crew captive.

programme.title: The Firm
programme.desc: A young lawyer joins a small but prestigious law firm only to find out that most of their clients are on the wrong side of the law. The company is helping to launder mob money, get clients off charges and even murder partners who threaten to blow their cover, but when the FBI come calling to gather evidence on the lawyer's colleagues, he is caught between a rock and a hard place, juggling his life and his liberty.

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Six government agents become trapped inside a high-security safe house after a terrorist attack in downtown Los Angeles. They soon realise they must survive not just the enemy outside, but the potential traitor among them.

programme.title: Red Dragon
programme.desc: Thriller adapted from Thomas Harris's first Hannibal Lecter novel about an FBI profiler called out of retirement to help catch a serial killer nicknamed the Tooth Fairy. The agent has the uncanny ability to project himself into the minds of his criminal prey, but the case forces him to confront his past and consult his former nemesis - the now-incarcerated Dr Hannibal Lecter.

programme.title: Hannibal
programme.desc: The outwardly charming and intelligent psychopath Dr Lecter is in Florence, masquerading as a museum curator. A greedy cop discovers his true identity, and plans to hand him over to someone willing to pay, but things do not go to plan when female FBI agent Clarice Starling gets a call to help Lecter's only living victim - the hideously deformed Mason Verger.

programme.title: The Equalizer
programme.desc: A former commando who had faked his own death comes out of retirement to rescue a young girl from violent Russian mobsters.

programme.title: The Equalizer 2
programme.desc: Robert McCall's mysterious past cuts especially close to home when thugs kill Susan Plummer, his best friend and former colleague. Now out for revenge, McCall must take on a crew of highly trained assassins who will stop at nothing to destroy him.

programme.desc: On August 7th 1974, at 7.15am, 24-year-old Frenchman Philippe Petit stepped off the edge of the South Tower of New York's World Trade Centre. The is the intimate story of Petit and his group of friends as they followed their dream.

At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Viking Age in Britain and visits excavations that are revealing a different side to these seafaring pirates from Scandinavia. programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and archaeologist Matt Williams present 2014's most outstanding archaeology from the north of Britain. Sites include the Ness of Brodgar, Maryport, Flixton in Yorkshire, and Ardnamurchan.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: This episode heads to the west of Britain, while archaeologists join us back in the Salisbury Museum to look at the new finds and what they mean.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the year's most exciting archaeological finds in the east of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy, and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the year's most exciting archaeological finds in the north of Britain. A team discovers clues to Scotland's first kingdoms, metal detectorists unearth a hoard of Viking treasure, and a new housing development reveals a graveyard of Iron Age warriors.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Finds include the lost WWI training trenches on Salisbury Plain, Britain's first `double henge', discovered just down the road from Stonehenge, where the evidence suggests our ancestors feasted and made sacred offerings as part of a visit to the ritualistic Stonehenge landscape, and luxury foreign goods discovered at Tintagel, the legendary childhood home of King Arthur.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Delving beneath the surface to discover the crucial role ships have played in international conflict over the past 500 years.

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: From soaring skyscrapers like One World Trade Center to impenetrable vaults like Fort Knox, humanity's strongest structures were engineered to defy disaster.

programme.desc: Was a UFO tracking an American fighter plane? What is the creature recorded undulating in waters off southeastern North Carolina? What made the strange howling sounds recorded at a truckstop along Highway 97?

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Viking Age in Britain and visits excavations that are revealing a different side to these seafaring pirates from Scandinavia. She looks for signs of the earliest Viking settlers in the Outer Hebrides, and in Orkney - where Viking dominance outlasted anywhere else in Britain - she visits the excavation of a Viking chief's citadel and finds evidence of their way-of-life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Ages of Bronze and Iron to discover what kind of a place Britain was before the Romans invaded. With no written history, only archaeology can provide the clues. Alice uncovers a world that is complex, sophisticated and pretty strange. She examines the two Hebridean Bronze Age skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Hazardous History With Henry Winkler
programme.desc: Ever taken medical advice from a cigarette commercial? There was a time when any and all cures could be sold to the public indiscriminately. Dive into a world where animal skin could replace your own, and where doctors gave coffee enemas.

programme.title: Hazardous History With Henry Winkler
programme.desc: The modern concept of extreme sports is a product of marketing, but back in the old days, the rules (or lack thereof) of our nation's sports made them truly extreme. From bone-crushing football and deadly baseball games to wild events like auto polo.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Investigators find a new clue about the Malaysia Airlines flight that disappeared in 2014. Plus, an astronaut spots something strange in the Sahara Desert.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A buried monument offers new evidence about a huge Biblical mystery. A giant shadow engulfs the Pacific Ocean, and strange circular lake is home to unearthly life.

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.title: Storage Wars
