programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
programme.desc: Dukat, demoted to freighter commander, and Kira join forces to restore Dukat's position by attacking the Klingons who destroyed a Cardassian outpost.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: An experimental procedure may free Teal'C of the need for a symbiote.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: McKay is reunited with his estranged sister Jeanie, who gave up her promising career to raise a family. Despite animosity between the two siblings, she agrees to help the SG-1 team with their work on a top-secret project.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Getting away from it all on the tropical resort of Risa, Picard is rudely interrupted by a security team from the 27th century - 300 years in the future - who enlist him in their search for a missing secret weapon.

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: Following an accident which leads to Tuvok and Neelix becoming a composite being, a doctor must try to work out a way of separating the two identities. Meanwhile, for Janeway there is a frightening decision to be made.

programme.title: FBI: Most Wanted
programme.desc: With COVID-19 devastating the country, two gunmen take out their desperation and rage on the elite class they perceive to be oppressing them; Jess' father returns and may not be over his old ways.

programme.title: FBI: Most Wanted
programme.desc: When a hacker triggers a car crash that kills a family of three, the team races to hunt him down as his ransomware demands escalate and his final target becomes apparent.

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: After an accident on the Enterprise, Archer loses his short-term memory and begins to wake up every day not remembering anything from the day before.

programme.title: Warehouse 13
programme.desc: A small town comes under threat as the team tries to track down the remaining artefacts from Sykes' arsenal.

programme.title: Brit Cops: War on Crime
programme.desc: Action-packed documentary series following Britain's cops in their war on crime.

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: Rosie and Daz are in Reading for `Operation Fanta' looking for burglars who steal car keys to get at the increasingly hard-to-nick cars.

programme.title: Highway Patrol
programme.desc: A driver is caught using his phone while on the road and launches into a profanity-laden rant. Also, Sergeant Ash Hodges comes across a man in a beaten up car who's leaving a crash scene.

programme.title: Border Patrol
programme.desc: New Zealand's Customs officers attempt to enforce the law and keep New Zealand's borders safe from harm.

programme.title: Wild Pacific Rescue
programme.sub-title: Battle to Save a Sea Lion
programme.desc: The Rescue team tries to save an otter as Dr Marty battles rough conditions to help an entangled sea lion. At the Rescue Centre, a mischievous seal pup puts himself in danger.

programme.title: Wild Pacific Rescue
programme.desc: The MMR team helps a lost sea turtle get back home. A huge sea lion is found shot with a cross bow bolt, and Dr Marty helps a fruit bat cope with old age.

programme.title: Bugs That Rule the World
programme.desc: The ruthless realm of some of the stealthiest and cleverest insects in the animal kingdom -- from praying mantises and aquatic insects to dragonflies.

programme.title: Bugs That Rule the World
programme.desc: The crucial ecological roles that beetles play in nearly every environment, and why they are often regarded "the most important species on the planet."

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Descend into the tide pool, where a hermit crab house hunts, a baby catshark runs into strange neighbours, and an octopus terrorises prey. Watch seagulls and other creatures hunt on the surface as the heat rises and oxygen depletes in the pool.

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Count down the seconds as the sun sets and predators prowl around the pool before the tide finally rolls in. Persevere as night falls with a kleptomaniacal slug and an acrobatic scallop, while a pair of sneaky crabs take a dangerous shot at romance.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Nearly every species on Earth must find a mate and while some become partners for life, for others it's just a fleeting moment.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: All airborne creatures embark on remarkable journeys through the air, some routes are short and others across hundreds of miles.

programme.title: Alien Abyss
programme.desc: Get acquainted with an array of ocean oddballs and learn how billions of years' worth of changes, from ice ages to asteroid strikes, have shaped their looks and skills. Meet blind worms who fish, walking sharks, tree-climbing crabs, and much more.

programme.title: Alien Abyss
programme.desc: Get acquainted with an array of ocean oddballs and learn how billions of years' worth of changes, from ice ages to asteroid strikes, have shaped their looks and skills. Meet blind worms who fish, walking sharks, tree-climbing crabs, and much more.

programme.title: Alien Abyss
programme.desc: Exploring the bizarre mating rituals of the ocean's most peculiar creatures. Learning how bloody battles for mates, gender bending, and super-size litters of babies might secure a next generation for these animals.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Wild Science uses CGI and research to reveal how animals survive, tracing their evolution and future across Earth's most extreme habitats.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough charts the story of animals who defy gravity, starting with the planet's first flyers: the insect.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: After meeting some critters who glide through life in Borneo, Sir David Attenborough decides to turn back the clock.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: In the final episode in the series, Sir David Attenborough turns his attention to the creatures that dominate our skies today.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Wild Science uses CGI and research to reveal how animals survive, tracing their evolution and future across Earth's most extreme habitats.

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Drought
programme.desc: In Southern Florida, a unique and complex ecosystem known as the Everglades has entered its winter season. Predator and prey are forced to live in close proximity due to a limited supply of water.

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Flood
programme.desc: Waters flood Southern Florida's mangroves and marshes. Many of the 600 animal species that live in the Everglades disperse during this time, expanding their hunting and breeding grounds.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: A rugged, sky-high plain in the remote heart of Bolivia, the Andean Altiplano lays claim to towering snow-capped peaks, glowing multi-coloured lagoons, and the world's largest salt desert.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: A diverse trek through the remote and surprising canyon lands of southern Utah reveals soaring cliff walls, forested plateaus, and breathtaking gorges.

programme.title: Wild Wild East
programme.desc: A look at the relationships between species like lobsters, scallops and crabs which are are numerous and complicated. Changing water temperature is bringing invasive species to the Wild Wild East, putting pressure on species humans count on.

programme.title: Wild Wild East
programme.desc: In the Northern Atlantic, cold Canadian waters surround eight European Islands. This is the last remaining overseas territory of France, and the summer-home to a semi-feral population of horses and ponies.

programme.title: Wild Wild East
programme.desc: Non-native species have made their way into the Wild Wild East and staked their claim to the jagged coast. At White Point Beach Resort in Liverpool, Nova Scotia bunnies have hopped on in for an extended holiday.

programme.title: Wild Wild East
programme.desc: In the woodland caribou of Gros Morne, the changing seasons offer a stern test for the wildlife. Even the arctic-ready muskoxen of north Quebec is challenged to find food in the whitest terrain.

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.desc: Deep in southern Africa, a mighty river snakes through untamed wilderness. Its vast flood plains are a magnet for wildlife. In the dry season animals travel great distances to reach this oasis and during droughts space and resources are at a premium.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Series following the residents of Dorset's Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre. Eighty-eight capuchin monkeys rescued from a research laboratory in Chile wake up to their first morning at Monkey World. Over in the Chimp nursery, Rodders has a run-in with a sticky melon, and the park's naughtiest resident, Seamus the chimp, comes face to face with alpha male Butch in the bachelor pad.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Chimps Ben and Pip are due to be moved out of the nursery and into Hananya's group - but they give wildlife vet John Lewis the run around when he tries to sedate them. Alison is worried about how the recently rescued male capuchins will react when they are moved in together, and orang-utan Kai proves she has far too much energy for the rest of her group.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Beni faces a day of reckoning as all the forest school students are held back for a weigh-in; Alba is back in isolation after being diagnosed with typhoid.

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: This episode visually illustrates how the Arctic, locked in darkness for nearly seven months, springs to life once the sun rises above the horizon. We explore ideas of migration, hibernation, the food web and habitat change.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: Kamuti, an old leopard, struggles to find a meal while hoping to avoid a pride of lions.

programme.title: Secrets of Wild Australia
programme.desc: At first glance, the tropical forests of Australia seem empty and still. But look closer and they are packed with life. Few places in the country contain such a high diversity of insects.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is invited to Switzerland on Valentine's Day to see the world's largest particle accelerator. Sheldon is gutted when Leonard asks Penny to go with him, as a Valentine's Day treat, instead of him. Sheldon tries to coerce the couple into letting him take Penny's place, will they relent?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is summoned to court for driving through a red light while taking Penny to hospital in an earlier episode. He misses meeting Stan Lee, creator of `Spiderman', at the comic book store and riles the judge so much that he ends up in prison. Penny feels so guilty that Sheldon has missed out on the opportunity to meet Stan Lee that she finds out his home address and takes Sheldon there.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang squabble over a ring which they believe was used in `The Lord of the Rings'. Leonard wants to give it back to its original owner, Koothrappali wants to sell it so that he can buy a jet ski but Sheldon becomes obsessed with the ring and starts to turn into Gollum from The Lord of the Rings. Can the guys reach an agreement and will Sheldon return to normal?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon has been awarded a prestigious prize by the university but is scared of collecting the gong at a ceremony because he knows he'll have to give a speech. Penny tries to gee Sheldon up using rousing references to `X-Men' and Leonard and Koothrappali offer psychological guidance without much success. On the night, Sheldon's nerves get the better of him and he has a drink before going on stage.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tells Penny that he loves her but doesn't get the response he would have liked. Elsewhere, the gang go bowling and get a surprise when Sheldon's enemy, `Star Trek' actor Wil Wheaton, is playing in the opposing team. Will Sheldon get revenge on Wil?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Blades of Glory

At the Winter Olympics, the fierce rivals find themselves forced to share the winner's podium when they both score gold. The shower of glory doesn't last for long, as the pair descend into fisticuffs and set the mascot on fire. Disgraced and stripped of their medals, they can get back on ice, but only if they dance with each other as a pair.

programme.title: Simon Reeve's South America
programme.desc: Simon visits Brazil, the biggest country on the continent. He starts in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon, with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: aul and Bob return to Scotland to fish for salmon once more, this time on the mighty River Tweed. They start their search for a so-called bar of silver surrounded by banks of lush tall trees at the Bemersyde Estate, a place where it is easy to forget the outside world and do nothing but concentrate on fishing.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul takes Bob on a trip down memory lane and back to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to come as a teenager with his mates. They would catch the train from north London to fish this network of rivers and canals that flow from Hertfordshire into the Thames, and just like when Paul was young, Paul and Bob fish for perch.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul revisit the River Wye in Herefordshire, this time they head to the spectacular Lower Wye to fish for chub. Bob has decided to meet Paul at the river and makes his entrance from upstream - on a rowing boat.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fly to Enniskillen to fish for Pike. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced local Frenchman Pascal. programme.title: Call the Midwife

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Drama series set in a Cheshire village during the Second World War. The isolated community could not feel further away from the impending bloodshed and battlefields, yet it is not immune from the effects of war, and everyone must make sacrifices as it begins to have an impact. Steph hides a secret that threatens the farm, Teresa's past comes back to haunt her and Erica is determined to intervene to protect Pat.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: The small hamlet of Botallack was once at the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry as archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers. By 1861, Botallack's mine was one of the biggest in the whole of Cornwall. Ben explores the village's mining past with archaeologist, Adam Sharpe, where he learns the mines produced millions and millions of pounds worth of copper and tin.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand was one of the last land-masses to be found and settled by people. Lush and fertile, almost everything brought here flourishes, often with surprising consequences. Told through the experiences of its native species - in particular, a charismatic and peculiar giant, flightless parrot - this is the moving story of the changing fortunes of New Zealand's wildlife, since humans first arrived.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: From pythons in Bangkok to otters in Singapore, a look at the extraordinary ways wildlife is making itself at home in the world's cities.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: As the urban world grows, animals are displaced from land that belonged to them. From herons to humpback whales, the stories of these wild outcasts are the most captivating of all.

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along The US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border, in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she tries just about every sample in a Tequila showroom and is left in no doubt that the city deserves its reputation as a party town. She also discovers an unexpected invasion of migrants: American retirees who have settled south of the border to enjoy the good life and an ocean view at an affordable price. Over on the US side of the border, Sue spends a day with Mark Lamb, sheriff of Pinal County.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Sitting on the east coast of Jersey, and about 14 miles from the coast of Normandy, the small village of Gorey was, for hundreds of years the frontline of defence between the British Isles and its enemy, the French. Archaeologist Ben Robinson follows the story of this small village's history as an important military location, from the castle built in the 1200s to the experiences of the village in the second world war when occupied by Hitler's army.

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: When Reverend Pottle, the 102-year-old vicar of Dibley village dies, the locals are shocked to discover that his replacement is a woman. programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey is appalled when her mother comes to visit her at university - especially when she begins to reminisce about her own student days. Meanwhile, Ben's plans for a relaxing round of golf do not go according to plan.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick wins an old car in a poker game, and intends to break it up for parts. Ben takes it over and decides to restore it to its former glory, but he is about as good an engineer as Isambard Kingdom Brunel was a dentist.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: It's Ben's birthday, and everyone seems to have taken his request for no fuss literally, neglecting to buy him any presents. Janey is home for the weekend with a new friend, but nobody is sure of the nature of their relationship.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When the children go away for the weekend, Ben and Susan finally have some time to themselves. Unfortunately, it's been so long since that happened, that they have absolutely no idea how to spend it.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: After signing up on a social networking site, Susan starts getting messages from an old flame. Meanwhile, Michael finds out their house is cursed and Abi is convince it is haunted.

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: One Flew Out of the Cuckoo's Nest
programme.desc: Ben gets his hopes up that finally one of his kids will move out when Nick decides to get his own flat. Unfortunately, as always for Ben, every silver lining has a cloud.

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A young family rush to the clinic with their Chihuahua, called Darcy, after she's hit by a car. Using pins, nuts and bolts, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick must reconstruct the broken pieces of Darcy's crushed pelvis.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The Warden family bring in their one-year-old Saint Bernard, Poppie, who's suffering with hip dysplasia, and a 13-year-old collie called Tess is rushed to Fitzpatrick's after being hit by a car.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A four-year-old Chinchilla requires surgery for a broken leg, but his tiny airways and bones make it a difficult procedure. A puppy called Coco is rushed to the practice after being hit by a van, and Professor Fitzpatrick fights to save her limb.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Tina and her boisterous sons bring in their springer spaniel puppy who has a badly deformed hind leg that requires complicated surgery. Professor Fitzpatrick also receives two-year-old cat Lily who drags one of her back legs.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Karen suffers from frequent nocturnal epileptic fits. Her Staffordshire Bull Terrier Pearl has the extraordinary ability to sense when these episodes are about to happen. But Pearl has problems with her knees and is unable to walk without pain.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The paramedics race to a lorry driver with a branch through his windscreen and a man with an irregular heartbeat, and help a family faced with a heart-breaking decision.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Following paramedics from the South Central Ambulance Service in Portsmouth and Oxford as they carry out their duties around Britain. Cameras are placed within ambulances, while the team wear body-mounted filming technology to capture all the action.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Sarah has an operation to repair the hernia she got after giving birth, while Geoffrey is desperate to save his sight after his retina becomes detached.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Seven-year-old Nancy has an operation to straighten her squint, and ex-Army man Mervyn who served in Afghanistan and Iraq, has a procedure to get rid of constant back pain.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Nan visits an old friend in hospital and leaves him a gift, Lauren takes a job at Billy's Burger Bar, and Geordie Georgie organises a fundraiser for battered husbands.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Coco, Sapphire and the Cheese Chis
programme.desc: Pete helps rescue six Chihuahuas from the home of a hoarder and witnesses the emotional handover of a pregnant pug and her sister.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: There is a suspected acid attack on the streets of Bradford.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: One final shift with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. A police chase ends with a spectacular crash and five trapped teens in the wreckage.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel faces one of his greatest ever practical and ethical challenges as he seeks to provide Hermes, the one-legged tortoise, with three new prosthetic feet. Also, a Rottweiler called Dave urgently needs an upgrade to his previous hip replacements.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw do battle in the antiques emporia of the south coast before an auction in Windsor. Paul plans to crush his opponent's lead with a tank but Kate fights back with a mechanical penguin.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners aid Sue, an artist who helped build a lesbian-safe section of Kansas City in the 80s, who hasn't been able to clean her "organized heaps" of art since the love of her life died of COVID-19.

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered
programme.desc: A detailed look at Aaron Hernandez's life, a millionaire athlete with a fiancé, baby and shockingly convicted of murder. New details and never-before-seen interviews explaining his troubled past and murder allegations.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Our Guy in China
programme.desc: Guy Martin explores Beijing, ignoring the tourist traps and following his own often eccentric passions. In a backstreet bicycle shop he spends £18 on a rudimentary `Flying Pigeon', the most produced machine in the world.

programme.title: Our Guy in China
programme.desc: Guy Martin concludes his Chinese adventure with his toughest ever physical challenge. He wants to break the record for the fastest crossing of the Taklamakan Desert on a bicycle.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Following his Appleton's guide, Michael Portillo makes a trip to the jewel in the crown of America's National Parks, Yosemite, to behold its towering granite cliffs, its giant sequoia trees and cascading waterfalls. He discovers how early conservationists fought to protect it in law and encounters a `buffalo soldier' in the heart of the forest. In Berkeley, Michael uncovers a catastrophic event, which hit the city of San Francisco hard in 1906.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his late 19th-century Appleton's guidebook, Michael Portillo continues through southern California aboard the Coast Starlight rail service. In the historic port of Monterey, Michael raises the Bear Flag of the one-time Republic of California and above it, the Stars and Stripes. He discovers the city's Spanish and Mexican heritage and traces how the former fishing port described as `stinking and decrepit' in John Steinbeck's novel, `Cannery Row', has become a beacon for tourists.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo strikes oil in the suburbs of Los Angeles, contemplates his navel in the orange groves of Riverside, makes a California roll and paints a pretty picture in Laguna Beach. It is Mexican Independence Day and the locals are celebrating the country's hard-won independence from Spain in the early 19th century.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie helps rescue a runaway narrowboat on the scenic Macclesfield Canal and cruises through autumn scenery toward Congleton to see a shipwreck up for sale.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Fallen trees block Robbie's journey on the Peak Forest Canal. Later, he meets TV host Terry Christian, whose dad once worked a local waterway.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock.

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer
programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels from the beautiful Pacific coast of Mexico, where he visits the luxurious holiday resorts of Baja California, before crossing the country's rugged interior. The first stage of his journey ends in the Bahamas where he discovers the hidden menace on the beautiful coral reefs.

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer
programme.desc: The second leg of the journey sees Simon dodge the Moroccan secret police in Western Sahara, travel on one of the world's longest trains in Mauritania, visit a forgotten refugee camp in the Algerian desert where more than 100,000 people live, and take a swim in a Libyan oasis with a government minder who bears an uncanny resemblance to Colonel Gadaffi.

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer
programme.desc: Simon rejoins the Tropic in Egypt and follows it through Saudi Arabia, Dubai, and Oman. In Southern Egypt, next to the ancient temple of Abu Simbal, Simon meets Nubians struggling to maintain their culture following the damming of the Nile and dives in the pristine reefs of the Red Sea.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie helps rescue a runaway narrowboat on the scenic Macclesfield Canal and cruises through autumn scenery toward Congleton to see a shipwreck up for sale.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Fallen trees block Robbie's journey on the Peak Forest Canal. Later, he meets TV host Terry Christian, whose dad once worked a local waterway.

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Frank struggles to look after himself while Betty is in the hospital, locking himself out and blowing himself up with a steak and kidney pudding; however, the fun really starts when he visits Betty.

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Convinced he is a failure, a depressed Frank visits a psychiatrist, but by the time Frank has related his life story, his shrink can only agree with his analysis.

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: The new manager of the labour exchange refuses to believe that Frank is unemployable.

programme.desc: The team take on one of Derek's favourites - an Austin A35 - but its age ensures some hefty bills. To bank some cash, their second project is a quick flip - a 1999 BMW Z3.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul and Charlie head south for an immaculate Lotus Super 7. Derek and Jack are in Sheffield collecting a 1929 Riley Monaco and a 1924 Alvis 12/50 tourer.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.desc: It's a first for the boys as they set their sights on a Saab. But when all hopes of a classic 99 are dashed, they're forced to settle for a failed rally car.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: The Japanese, at war on the Chinese mainland since 1931, hope for quick victories over the British and the Dutch, but 7 December 1941 brings the infamous attack on Pearl Harbor, and the start of an all-conquering sweep through Southeast Asia culminating in the taking of Singapore.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: In Poland lies a forgotten Nazi complex which could have sheltered up to 27,244 people from Allied bombs, the purpose of which largely remains a mystery.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts welcome thousands of visitors to the gardens of Tatton Park in Cheshire. Henry Sandon has a memorable day when one of the rarest 18th century pieces of Royal Worcester turns up. Other finds include a box of the world's most valuable and beautiful diamonds, that aren't quite what they appear to be, and an old box of golf balls with a value that's definitely not below par.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts pay a return visit to Tatton Park in Cheshire. They uncover more objects and stories rich in history, including a silver inkstand reputed to be given to Admiral Nelson by his lover, Lady Emma Hamilton, in 1805; and an oak coffer that arrives via an unusual mode of transport. Plus, John Benjamin finds an unlikely-looking gem lurking within a box of costume jewellery.
